issue_title: Mixing mainnet and testnet addresses in outputs
issue_body:
Bitcoin mainnet and testnet addresses encode the same information/scripts, but have different prefixes to differentiate between the two networks.

Currently in Bit it is possible to e.g. spend from a mainnet address/PrivateKey but include testnet addresses in the outputs. Bit then simply converts those testnet addresses to its mainnet equivalent in the outputs of the spending transaction. 

This may lead to confusion, and I would like to discuss if maybe there should be some sanity checks in place to let the user know when a mix of mainnet and testnet addresses and private keys are used?

issue_title: Shortcuts module to easy use
issue_body:
Hello. With string interpolation on Python3.6 it's really easy to use colors with print, but it may be even less verbose, even if it's much better than colorama in that way.
Maybe it will be good choice to support some shortcuts module?
For example, 
```python
import sty.shortcuts as f
print(f'Hello, {f.bred}hello{f.rs}')
```
f.bred = ef.b + fg.red
f.ured = ef.u + fg.red
f.ired = ef.i + fg.red,
f.rs = rs.all
f.xgreen = bg.green

I mean - I understand that we're talking about couple less symbols, but it would be very cool when you're hard working with colored output, plus - we have only one var in namespace.

issue_title: New label sometimes destroys hex alignment (edit in hex)
print_format parameter throws error
issue_body:
Thanks for contributing 👍 

Using this:

```
ff_probe = ffmpy.FFprobe(
    inputs = {
        'myurl': None,
    },
    global_options = [
        '-print_format json',
        '-show_format',
        '-show_streams',
    ]
)
```

Runs this command:
`ffprobe "-print_format json" -show_format -show_streams -i http://myurl.me`

And gives me this error:
`Failed to set value '-show_format' for option 'print_format json': Option not found`

As you can see the *print_format json* gets wrapped in double quotes.

issue_title: Argument 1 must be string, not None when creating TornadoSession
issue_body:
I'm trying to use TornadoSession in my project, but I can't make it work.
When the function TornadoSession() is called it raise the following exception:

` Exception in callback <functools.partial object at 0x7fb710f9c2b8>
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/tornado/ioloop.py", line 600, in _run_callback
        ret = callback()
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/tornado/stack_context.py", line 275, in null_wrapper
        return fn(*args, **kwargs)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/queries/tornado_session.py", line 374, in on_connected
        cursor = self._get_cursor(conn)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/queries/session.py", line 311, in _get_cursor
        cursor_factory=self._cursor_factory)
    TypeError: argument 1 must be string, not None`

It gives this error even with one of the example found in the documentation:

```
from tornado import gen, ioloop, web
import queries

class MainHandler(web.RequestHandler):

    def initialize(self):
		conf = queries.uri(host='localhost', port=5432, dbname="myDb", user="root", password="pass")
		self.session = queries.TornadoSession(conf)

    @gen.coroutine
    def get(self):
        results = yield self.session.query('SELECT * FROM myTable')
        self.finish({'data': results.items()})
        results.free()
application = web.Application([
    (r"/", MainHandler),
])

if __name__ == "__main__":
    application.listen(8888)
    ioloop.IOLoop.instance().start()
```

What can cause this issue?

issue_title: Required version of Six incorrect
issue_body: Hi, requirements.txt says it needs Six version 1.7 or greater, but it looks like you are using the raise_from() method which, as far as I can tell, wasn't introduced until Six version 1.9.

issue_title: Show severity levels directly in the output
issue_body:
Hi first of all thanks for your linter :+1: 

Without using json output, its hard to get severity level (error, warning ...), so can we have a way to output it directly?

For example ...
```
sourcePreview.vim:110:16: unexpected token: <EOL> (see ynkdir/vim-vimlparser)
```

... will become something like this:
```
sourcePreview.vim:110:16: error: unexpected token: <EOL> (see ynkdir/vim-vimlparser)
```

Its will be easier then to integrate `vint` into external tools.

issue_title: Spurious overflow error in bincoef
Add --cache-only flag
issue_body: Instead of having CI run the full mutation stage we could check in the entire mutmut cache dir and in CI run with a a --cache-only flag where it fails if the cache is out of date. This would make it nice to have a mutation testing badge on your project on github, but without the hassle of storing and loading the mutmut cache database or the cost of running the full mutation testing on travis on each commit.

issue_body:
If I define a custom unit which also happens to be a Python reserved word, its parsing lead to an error.

Here is an example with a custom `try` unit:
```python
>>> from pint import UnitRegistry
>>> ureg = UnitRegistry()
>>> ureg.define('try = []')
>>> ureg.parse_expression('try')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/kdeldycke/virtualenvs/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pint/unit.py", line 1258, in parse_expression
    values
  File "<string>", line 1
    Q_ (1 ,U_ (try=1 ))
                 ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax
```

Just wondering then if the `eval` used in `parse_expression()` can be exploited for remote execution if definition of custom units is exposed to users.

issue_title: Python 3 support
issue_body:
_From [johann....@gmail.com](https://code.google.com/u/118084747536112177642/) on September 25, 2011 11:02:57_

Attached is a patch for python3 support.
The patch is not pretty and not perfect, but it works for me.

Should work with versions 2.6-3.2, though i only tested it with python2.7 and python3.2. For versions < 2.6 there'd be a lot more work to do.

API break for py3k:
you have to use for example:
a = IP(b'asd')
b = bytes(a)
assert a == IP(b)

For py2 it should remain the same as before.

**Attachment:** [py3.patch](http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=76)

_Original issue: http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=76_

issue_title: Assertion `op >= 0 && op <= 2' failed when -S tag passed
issue_body:
Hello, I'm getting an error when passing the -S tag. I can't figure out what exactly is going wrong
My command:
`minimap2 -x splice -S -k 14 -N 0 -t 24 ce11.fa  170206_celegans_wtadult_rna.111.fa  > albacore_rna_MINIMAP2_ce11_genome_all_rna_k14.paf 2> albacore_rna_MINIMAP2_ce11_genome_all_rna_k14{kmer}.log`
And the logfile:
>[M::mm_idx_gen::5.103*0.97] collected minimizers

issue_title: Import plugins from other plugins?
issue_body:
Is it possible to import another plugin from a plugin?

* plugins/
    * helper_plugin.py
    * job_plugin.py

```
#helper_plugin.py
from will.plugin import WillPlugin
from will.decorators import respond_to, periodic, hear, randomly, route, rendered_template, require_settings
import random

class HelperPlugin(WillPlugin):
    def generate_number(self):
        return random.randint(0,100)
```

```
#job_plugin.py
from will.plugin import WillPlugin
from will.decorators import respond_to, periodic, hear, randomly, route, rendered_template, require_settings
from helper_plugin import HelperPlugin

class JobPlugin(WillPlugin):
    def do_job(self):
        print "Random number is {}".format(HelperPlugin.generate_number())
```

This is an example of what I want. job_plugin fails on startup saying that there's no module named helper_plugin

issue_title: Submodules
issue_body: Seeing as ifort now supports [submodules](http://fortranwiki.org/fortran/show/Submodules) and it looks like gfortran [isn't far behind](https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=52846#c2), it might be worth making FoBiS aware of them and how the affect dependencies and the build order. (Just something that was on my mind since I want to implement support for them in FORD before the next release.)

issue_title: Bad hang then stalls and weirdness
issue_body:
### Step 1: Describe your environment

  * System (Intel/AMD/Raspberry Pi etc): Intel i7-855OU, X1 Carbon G6
  * OS version: Linux Mint 18.3 Cinnamon
  * s-tui version: Whatever the pip install gives you
  * Installation method(pip/PPA/source code): pip install
  
### Step 2: Describe the problem:
#### Observed Results:

  * What happened? (Traceback of a crash/Image/description):

First test: after running for a few seconds, and possibly dependent upon user keypresses, the program hung the whole system hard, such that I have to Raise Elephants. I don't think I could have run the program's stress test tool - by accident - because that tool requires separate installation. I note that when I installed the program I did not install the `python-dev` package, though I later discovered that I lacked the package and installed that package. 

Second test - after installing python-dev - the program would, every few minutes, stall noticeably, but then recover and the rest of the system seemed unaffected, except that, even after I'd closed the program, the top of my screen would flicker occasionally in an alarming way. issue_title: IOError: [Errno 61] No data available
issue_body:
On my Ubuntu 17.04, I get a Traceback, with `IOError: [Errno 61] No data available`. This happens for the pip and the github version.
A `--help` works.

Any help / tips?


```
sander@Stream-13:~$ sudo s-tui 
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/s-tui", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('s-tui==0.5.0', 'console_scripts', 's-tui')()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/s_tui/s_tui.py", line 767, in main
    graph_controller = GraphController(args)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/s_tui/s_tui.py", line 617, in __init__
    self.data = GraphData(is_admin=is_admin)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/s_tui/GraphData.py", line 60, in __init__
    self.rapl_power_reader = RaplPower()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/s_tui/RaplPower.py", line 24, in __init__
    self.max_power = self.read_max_power_file() / self.MICRO_JAUL_IN_JAUL
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/s_tui/RaplPower.py", line 35, in read_max_power_file
    return float(self.read_measurement(self.intel_rapl_package_max_energy_file))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/s_tui/RaplPower.py", line 28, in read_measurement
    value = file.read()
IOError: [Errno 61] No data available
sander@Stream-13:~$ 
```
and

```

    main()

    self.data = GraphData(is_admin=is_admin)

issue_title: Improve Green's own Unit Tests
issue_body:
Areas that could be improved:

* Use only one style of docstring
* Leverage helper functions and `textwrap.dedent()` to reduce the amount of boilerplate code in tests where we write external test files to load and run.
* Use newer testing facilities like `addCleanup` and context handlers.

issue_title: Entity registrations are not reflected in home assistant
issue_body:
If a light/switch/sensor is added to the deconz network while home assistant is already running; it doesn't get reported. Home-assistant needs to be restarted for it to recognize the newly added component.

This is particularly painful, if deconz and home-assistant are started one after the other. Because the deconz rest plugin does not report any lights unless it has been discovered first. (even if they are already registered in the db to belong to the network) As a result, any component that was not discovered yet during startup, will never show on home-assistant.

projectdb.update(project, utils.unicode_obj(info))

projectdb.update(project, utils.unicode_obj(info))

engine = create_engine(self.url, convert_unicode=True, pool_recycle=3600)

issue_title: Long running ldap connections
issue_body:
I am running a webapp which needs to perform ldap operations. There are two error sources:

1. The ldap-backend is down sometimes 
2. Long running connections without traffic get thrown away by firewall

My actual aproach is: For any operation I create a new connection object, perform a bind and if it fails the frontend gives an error to the user. The downside:

1. expensive binds are performed all the time
2. I can't use 'get_info=SCHEMA' as it would be even more expensive

What I'd rather have is one global connection which:

- always tries to perform an ldapsearch
- if the server does not send any data, it performs a rebind
- if the rebind fails it sends an error

What I tried is:
    
```
  #serverpool with 3 servers (same)
servers = [Server("myserver", connect_timeout=2)]
server_pool = ServerPool(servers=servers, exhaust=10)

con = Connection(user="me", password="pass", server=server_pool, pool_keepalive=10, receive_timeout=10, auto_bind=True, client_strategy=RESTARTABLE)
```

What now happens is:
- `con.bound` is always `True` even when the server is down 
- if `myserver` is not accessible and i perform `con.search(...)`, it does not give any error (means, it waits for the connection to be reestablished).

How do I establish just a single connection which automatically rebinds if the server is gone or the connection is not active anymore?

Thanks a lot (sorry for that much text)

issue_title: Introspection does not handle well exported objects with a common prefix in path
issue_body:
The introspection of txdbus does not handle correctly objects whose path have a prefix in common.

Just having any parts in common causes every entry to be duplicated in the generated XML.

Example:

```
from twisted.internet import reactor, defer

from txdbus import client
from txdbus.objects import DBusObject


@defer.inlineCallbacks
def main():
    conn = yield client.connect(reactor)
    conn.exportObject(DBusObject('/org/example/ObjPath'))
    conn.exportObject(DBusObject('/org/example/ObjPath2'))

    yield conn.requestBusName('org.example')


reactor.callWhenRunning(main)
reactor.run()
```

Introspection XML for path `/`:

```
<node name="/">
<node name="org"/>
<node name="org"/>
</node>
```

Introspection XML for path `/org/`:

```
<node name="/org/">
<node name="example"/>
<node name="example"/>
</node>
```

-

Having one object whose path is "under" another object's path excludes the former from the introspection.

```
from twisted.internet import reactor, defer

from txdbus import client
from txdbus.objects import DBusObject


@defer.inlineCallbacks
def main():
    conn = yield client.connect(reactor)
    conn.exportObject(DBusObject('/org/example/ObjPath'))
    conn.exportObject(DBusObject('/org/example/ObjPath/more_path'))
    yield conn.requestBusName('org.example')


reactor.callWhenRunning(main)
reactor.run()
```

Introspection XML for path `/org/example/ObjPath`: (Snipped for brevity; the issue is that it should have had a `<node name="more_path"/>` under the root element.)

```
<node name="/org/example/ObjPath">
  <interface name="org.freedesktop.DBus.Properties">
    [...]
  </interface>
  <interface name="org.freedesktop.DBus.Introspectable">
    [...]
  </interface>
  <interface name="org.freedesktop.DBus.Peer">
    [...]
  </interface>
  <interface name="org.freedesktop.DBus.ObjectManager">
    [...]
  </interface>
</node>
```
-

If fixes are welcome, I can make a pull request.

issue_title: Face detection on Video frames is very slow in Python
issue_body:
I have installed and compiled dlib on windows 10 with AVX_Instructions enabled to make code faster. Additionally, when building the project in Visual Studio I compiled it with the release mode x64. 
I tested the c++ version of the code and is working perfectly and very fast.

Inside the dlib directory I write the following in cmd: 
python setup.py install --yes USE_AVX_INSTRUCTIONS

But, when testing the python version of the code with a video as input, mainly face detection code, face detection was too slow, requiring around 170 ms for each frame. 

Please note that I am reading the frames with opencv3.1.
I have anaconda and python3.

issue_title: 1.6.1 --> 1.6.3 introduced breaking API changes: .decode(token=) vs .decode(jwt=)
issue_body:
PR #344 renamed some arguments on `api_jwt` ([for example](https://github.com/jpadilla/pyjwt/pull/344/files#diff-00ea5fc2260636f8d87435daf8c3b4c7R69)).  This is a breaking API change -- although the argument normalization is appreciated and an improvement, this should have triggered a major version bump (eg 1.6.1 --> 2.0.0) or there should have been a deprecation warning when using the old arg.

## Expected Result

[Semver](https://semver.org/) states:

> Given a version number MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, increment the:
> MAJOR version when you make incompatible API changes,
> MINOR version when you add functionality in a backwards-compatible manner, and
> PATCH version when you make backwards-compatible bug fixes.

My dependency manager matches pyjwt on 1.6.*, so I expected a patch version bump to be not breaking.

Given the [wide usage](https://libraries.io/pypi/PyJWT/usage) of PyJWT, API should be stable across patch releases.

## Actual Result

My dependency manager pulled in 1.6.3, and suddenly my build broke.

issue_body:
>>> from blessed import Terminal
    >>> term = Terminal()
    >>> text = u'\x1b[1m\x1b[30m13:55\x1b[m \x1b[1m\x1b[30m-\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m*\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m\x1b[30m-\x1b[m\x1b[m Use \x1b[1m/QUIT\x1b[m\x1b[m to quit. Other commands: \x1b[1m/HELP\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m, \x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m/ME\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m, \x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m/TOPIC\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m, \x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m/NAMES\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[m'
    >>> term.wrap(text, 46)
    [u'\x1b[1m\x1b[30m13:55\x1b[m \x1b[1m\x1b[30m-\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m*\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m\x1b[30m-\x1b[m\x1b[m Use \x1b[1m/QUIT\x1b[m\x1b[m to quit.',
    u'Other commands: \x1b[1m/HELP\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m, \x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m/ME\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m,',
    u'\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m/TOPIC\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[37m, \x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[1m/NAMES\x1b[m\x1b[m\x1b[m']

I added line breaks for clarity in the output of term.wrap. Here's what it winds up wrapping to:

    13:55 -*- Use /QUIT to quit.
    Other commands: /HELP, /ME,
    /TOPIC, /NAMES

The first line is 28 characters. Even by adding ' Other', it would only be 34 characters--well within the limit of 46 that I'm using in the example. The second line is 27 characters. By adding ' /TOPIC,', it would only be 32 characters--again, within the limit of 46.

I'm wondering if this is because the output is messy and has a lot of repeated `\x1b[1m` sequences...

issue_title: Simplification of long short strategy like in SIT R
issue_body:
I have used systemic investor toolbok (SIT) package in R and its very good for backtesting. In that package creating signal for long, short  or long-shortstrategy is [very easy](https://inovancetech.com/backtesttutorialr.html).

```
#long only strategy
signal<-ifelse(RSI3 < 30 & CCI20 > -290 & CCI20 < -100 & DEMA10c > -40 & DEMA10c < -20,1,NA)

#short only strategy
signal<-ifelse(RSI3 >30 & CCI20 > -290 & CCI20 > -100 & DEMA10c < -40 & DEMA10c > -20,-1,NA)

#long and sort strategy
buy.signal<-ifelse(RSI3 < 30 & CCI20 > -290 & CCI20 < -100 & DEMA10c > -40 & DEMA10c < -20,1,-1)

```

and then pass the signal to a backtest function

```
data$weight[] = NA
data$weight[] = signal
models$result = bt.run.share(data, clean.signal=T, trade.summary = TRUE)
```
It would be easier if **bt** also has similar simplified functions.

issue_title: Elevation calculation
issue_body:
I realized that the calculation of the elevation (uphill, downhill values) differs quite a lot from other tools. In my case I use the [movescount.com](http://movescount.com) webpage and [GPSMaster](http://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/GpsMaster) and the values are significant higher than the gpxpy values.

The reason for this is that the elevations are smoothed in the calculations. When removing the smoothed part I get similar values as with the other tools. see [https://github.com/svendroid/gpxpy/commit/7af065b71fd2687cf9e8a27b4ff1bb84200f5913](https://github.com/svendroid/gpxpy/commit/7af065b71fd2687cf9e8a27b4ff1bb84200f5913).

Why is the smoothing done? Is it more accurate than just adding the differences? Could you maybe give me a link to more information about this approach?

Thanks a lot for the nice lib,
Sven

issue_title: Compiler emits absolute paths to schemas, preventing sdist usage on Windows
issue_body:
If `setup()` for `myapp` includes

    capnpy_schemas=['myapp/schema.capnp'],

and I run `python setup.py sdist`, then in `myapp.egg-info/SOURCES.txt` I have

    /absolute/path/to/myapp/schema.capnp

which breaks installation on non-unix platforms because `distutils.util.convert_path` throws `ValueError` for absolute paths.

I think this would be fixed if `capnpy.compiler.distutils.capnpify` emitted `Extension` objects with relative (rather than absolute) paths to the schemas.

issue_title: Google Summer of Code
issue_body:
Hey @myint and @hhatto specifically as you're listed as package owner on PyPI.

[coala](https://coala.io) will most likely participate in GSoC this year. [We are using autopep8](https://github.com/coala/bear-docs/blob/master/docs/PEP8Bear.rst) to power part of the python code analysis we offer in coala.

Now here's the thing: would you, i.e. autopep8, be willing to take on a GSoC student we might be able to get you one, functioning as an umbrella organization.

## How it works

- You define a project with us.
- You provide at least one mentor, two ideally. 
- One member of coala will be paid for administering coala, that includes:
    - helping you making a great idea
    - helping you with any problems you might have
    - checking up on all students, finding out if mentors fail to do so themselves :)

## Why do it

- You get a student that works on your project, ideally sticks with your project long term
- You don't have to bother about the admin work
- Our communities can share knowledge

## Why we do it

- We get better integration of your tool
- We get upstream improvements
- We get another student, google likes umbrella orgs as they are their chance to pull in more small projects like you
- Our communities can share knowledge

Does this sound interesting to you? We will submit the initial application somewhen tomorrow, the deadline is later. However **we need to know if this is going to happen within the next week sharp.**

Feature Request
issue_body:
Have you thought of adding a "reload" method to the Netmiko library?  For example, "net_connect.reload()", which would send a "reload" command down the channel, expecting this prompt:
 
![capture](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/23405619/21285500/e2bfa8fe-c3ff-11e6-9c41-85d0bf7a905e.PNG)

And then sending "y" down the channel to initiate the reboot.  I've been playing around with automating switch firmware upgrades and have run into a little snag with this prompt after issuing the "reload" command.  

Interestingly, this does work if the code is ran in the Python interpreter and if a keyboard interrupt is issued after each "reload" and "y" command is sent using the send_command method.  For example, my code:

issue_title: _check_tunnel fails when using adresses other than 127.0.0.1
issue_body:
Hello, 

First of all thank you for this great library !!

In `_check_tunnel(self, _srv)`, to avoid Windows error when trying to connect to `0.0.0.0`, use of `127.0.0.1` is forced:

```python
# Windows raises WinError 10049 if trying to connect to `0.0.0.0`
s.connect(('127.0.0.1', _srv.local_port))
```
When setting up tunnels on addresses picked from `127.0.0.1/8` (loopback ip addres range) (e.g. `127.1.1.1`), `_check_tunnel(self, _srv)` still uses `127.0.0.1` instead of `127.1.1.1` which gives wrong result (a `DOWN` status instead of an `UP`)

Following code would allow to correctly check those tunnels:

```python
# Windows raises WinError 10049 if trying to connect to 0.0.0.0
addr = '127.0.0.1' if _srv.local_host == '0.0.0.0' else _srv.local_host
s.connect((addr, _srv.local_port))
```

Regards

issue_title: interval validation: is empty okay?
issue_body:
I'm running into validation failures in some segment data due to [this check](https://github.com/craffel/mir_eval/blob/master/mir_eval/util.py#L708-L710).  I can't remember why we require non-empty intervals, but I don't think there's a technical reason for it in terms of div-by-zero errors in any of the metrics.  I suspect this came out of some of the weirder conversion errors in parsing the salami dataset.

Could we relax this check to instead fail only if `intervals[:, 1] < intervals[:, 0]` (negative duration) and throw a warning if they're equal?

issue_title: Getting the inferred type of a variable?
issue_body:
I'm wondering if it is possible to easily get the type of a variable at the cursor. I'm able to successfully get the completions of a string type and a type inferred through a docstr. Is it possible simply get the inferred variable type?

I have a small test script: 

    import jedi
    import utool as ut
    source = r'''
        def spam(ibs):
            r"""
            Args:
                ibs (ibeis.IBEISController): an object
            """
            import jedi
            jedi.n
            x = ''
            x.l
            ibs.d
        '''
    )
    script = jedi.Script(source, line=9)
    script.completions()
    self = script = jedi.Script(source, line=10)  # NOQA
    script.completions()
    self = script = jedi.Script(source, line=10, column=7)  # NOQA
    script.completions()

(In the above script I use Google style docstrs. I'm using a checkout of jedi that has merged the pull request from https://github.com/WoLpH/jedi to support Google style docstrs. So, completions are working for me in this example.)

I have been able to do some hacking to get what I need by getting the completions and then getting the parent of any functions. 

    cs = script.completions()
    print([c.type for c in cs])
    funcs = [c for c in cs if c.type.startswith('function')]
    f = funcs[0]
    vartype = f.parent().name

This method seems hacky and error prone. I was wondering if there was a better way to do this, or if support for doing this might be added in the future?

issue_title: pycparser increases memory usage of pyOpenSSL by an order of magnitue
issue_body:
I have opened https://github.com/pyca/pyopenssl/issues/202 which shows the rewrite of pyOpenSSL from C bindings to it's current usage of "cryptography" (the package) between 0.13 & 0.14 has increased memory usage by an order of magnitude from ~2mb to ~20mb

The issue was debugged from issues with openstack ci jobs running out of memory in [1]

As described there, heapy showed the top heap usage as

```
Partition of a set of 205366 objects. Total size = 30969040 bytes.
 Index  Count   %     Size   % Cumulative  % Kind (class / dict of class)
     0  67041  33  5712560  18   5712560  18 str
     1  10260   5  2872800   9   8585360  28 dict of pycparser.plyparser.Coord
     2  27765  14  2367552   8  10952912  35 tuple
     3   1215   1  2246760   7  13199672  43 dict (no owner)
     4   1882   1  1972336   6  15172008  49 dict of pycparser.c_ast.Decl
     5  16085   8  1736232   6  16908240  55 list
     6    360   0  1135296   4  18043536  58 dict of module
     7   4041   2  1131480   4  19175016  62 dict of pycparser.c_ast.TypeDecl
     8   4021   2  1125880   4  20300896  66 dict of pycparser.c_ast.IdentifierType
     9   6984   3   893952   3  21194848  68 types.CodeType
<413 more rows. Type e.g. '_.more' to view.>
```

which seems to indicate pycparser as pretty uch the top consumer.  Of course there are many layers here -- pyOpenSSL -> cryptography -> cffi -> pycparser

I am not sure if something is tickling a bug in pycparser and causing leakage, or if this is just expected usage.  The increase is rather dramatic however.

I understand this isn't a great bug report.  I haven't a regression point where things suddenly started using more memory and it may be something one of the upper layers is doing wrong.

[1] https://etherpad.openstack.org/p/oom-in-rax-centos7-CI-job

issue_title: Reconnect timeout
issue_body:
I'm using telepot to run a bot on Raspberry Pi that controls and reports heating in my house.
Issue is that internet connection is not reliable there and sometimes it goes offline (and downtime could be hours). Telepot seems to handle these cases pretty well, but the moment internet goes off it starts infinitely retrying to connect, causing high CPU load. Which is not good as far as other tasks are also running in the background and there are no real reason to do these attempts without timeout in my case.
Is there is a way to configure retry attempts to have 1..5 seconds delay between attempts?
Would be great to have this delay to be an option for **bot.message_loop()**

issue_title: IPDB: high memory usage with many addresses

We have a long-running process running pyroute2 which is seeing memory usage issues. The hosts this process runs on have an unusual number of IP addresses (the vast majority are IPv6):

    # ip addr | grep "inet" | wc -l

The rest of the network setup is not remarkable.

The first issue is that merely instantiating `IPDB()` causes the Python process to jump in size by around 350MB. A heapy dump of the process shows the following heap usage profile:

issue_title: Support for aliases for release groups to increase matching
issue_body:
A similar use case would be aliases for release groups.
Certain subtitles of 1 release group will always be in sync with another release group (example: LOL and DIMENSION)
If aliases could be defined on release group level, it would also improve the matching.
This is certainly useful where subliminal is used in library mode and certain criteria must match before a subtitle is downloaded. If for example a subtitle is available under 'LOL' but the not under 'DIMENSION', it would be nice that 'DIMENSION' is also matched for 'LOL'. 
So making it possible to provide 'aliases' for certain video fields would be a good idea.

issue_title: User as Channel inconsistency in send_message vs wait_for_message
issue_body:
For `send_message`, you are allowed to pass in a `User` for `destination` and have it interpreted as a `PrivateChannel` between that user and the client automatically. However for `wait_for_message`, you pass in a `User` for the `channel` parameter and it will hang forever - you must pass in the `PrivateChannel` to match.

It would be nice if the same interpretation of a `User` in `send_message` also applied to `wait_for_message`.

issue_title: Multiple version information entries
issue_body:
**Problem:**
Some PE files may have multiple VS_VERSION_INFO entries. However, currently pefile only parses the first entry found.

The problem arises due to this line:
https://github.com/erocarrera/pefile/blob/29d49621d19bf885f90af267146a204523d10b0f/pefile.py#L3101-L3106

Possible change:
```
for rsrc_entry in last_entry.directory.entries[0].directory.entries:
    rt_version_struct = None
    try:
        rt_version_struct = rsrc_entry.data.struct
    except:
        # Maybe a malformed directory structure...?
        # Lets ignore it
        pass

    if rt_version_struct is not None:
        self.parse_version_information(rt_version_struct)
```

However, if we want to dump multiple version information, further changes in `parse_version_information()` is required, since `self.FileInfo` is re-initialized inside it. Otherwise, the previous version information parsed will be overwritten on the next parsing iteration.
Therefore, `self.FileInfo` will need to be re-structured if we want to apply the fix above in order to dump multiple version information.

I haven't been able to find a reference on how Windows decides exactly which version information gets displayed in _Properties_. If someone finds a reference to it please kindly share it here.

**File information from VirusTotal (malware):**
https://www.virustotal.com/#/file/392f40778b3a67601e6a00021c2f8ee715b23ef50298a5d73c033bea34cd837c/detection

I'm not quite sure what the breaking change was intending to do, so I'm not quite sure what an appropriate patch would be. I think it's something to do with local development based on [the release notes of 2.0](https://github.com/evansd/whitenoise/blob/master/CHANGELOG.rst#v20).

issue_title: Deserialize Method
issue_body:
# Feature
Add a `.is_valid_msg()` method to the `IFuzzable` interface.

The method should return True if the data given to it can be parsed as a valid message; false otherwise.

# Motivation
A simple way to improve the thoroughness of a fuzzer is to parse the reply messages received to ensure they meet expected values.

# Design
I can think of three steps to implementation:

1. The easiest step would be to use the `original_value` property. The data to be checked could be compared with the `Request` object's `original_value.
2. For further customization, we could add a `valid_values` parameter to fuzz primitives. This parameter would be a list of valid values that the primitive could take.
3. A list of values may be too much for certain data types, so a deserialization function could be given to customize behavior. This is not too far from creating a new fuzz primitive type.

Fuzzing activities would also be helped by a `deserialize()` method, providing an object representation of the message. This object could be used to check various properties of the reply. That might be more than is needed typically, and comes close to allowing for a full protocol functional test, perhaps beyond the needs of a fuzzer.

For now, a procedure that parses a message for validity might be enough. A typical `post_send` method could parse against the expected message and a range of known error messages. Any message following outside this domain could be considered erroneous.

issue_title: Client API?

Would this package consider supplying a client API similar to [this one](https://github.com/gerold-penz/python-jsonrpc#http-client-example)?

If the `getaddr` tricks give people the willies, an alternative might be to explicitly initialize each remote method, for example:

```
from jsonrpc import Remote

# Optional kwargs for jsonrpc, headers
my_remote = Remote(url)
my_remote.add_method('echo')
my_remote.add_method('ping', notify=True)

response = my_remote.echo('foobar')
print response.result

my_remote.ping()  # returns None
```

issue_title: Parameter in the uri segment matters.
issue_body:
Hi,

I faced a problem when I tried to get summary statement under paths.
Sample swagger snippet is following.

```
   /api/v1/search/:
        post:
            summary: search
            description: |
               search something
            consumes:
                - application/json
            produces:
                - application/json
            responses:
                200:
                    description: success
                500:
                    description: failure

    /api/v1/{vxlan_id}/:
        get:
            summary: id check
            description: |
                Check id.
            consumes:
                - application/json
            produces:
                - application/json
            parameters:
                - name: vxlan_id
                  in: path
                  description: logical_switch_id
                  required: true
                  type: string
                  pattern: ^[a-z]{1}[a-z0-9\-_]{0,63}$
                  maxLength: 64
                  minLength: 1
            responses:
                200:
                    description: success
                500:
                    description: failure
```

When I want to know the summary statement of '/api/v1/search/',
I use match_path_to_api_path('/api/v1/search/') to get the statements under '/api/v1/search/:'.

However, match_path_to_api_path('/api/v1/search/') returns '/api/v1/{vxlan_id}/' 
because maybe '/api/v1/{vxlan_id}/' is longer than '/api/v1/search/' by 4 characters.

I don't know the specification of swagger well, but simple comparison should 
take priority over regular expression comparison, I think.

I'm not accustomed to using English, so there might be problems to understand.
In the case, please ask a question.

To solve this issue I made a PR.

Regards,

Decoupling for microservice architectures
issue_body:
Currently, the `sanic-jwt` model is not ideal for microservice architecture. The module requires that it be able to `authenticate` a user and that has the `/auth` endpoints setup. However, in a microservice setup, likely there will only be one service that needs this functionality. The rest would need to have only the `@protected` and `@scoped` decorators.

Therefore, we should introduce a new configuration to enable this. Something like this:

```python
Initialize(app, auth_mode=False)
```

If `auth_mode` is **off**, I do not get an error for forgetting to pass `authenticate`.

    sanic_jwt.exceptions.AuthenticateNotImplemented: Sanic JWT initialized without providing an authenticate method.

And, it does not instantiate the blueprint, so that instance does not get any of the `/auth` endpoints.

issue_title: Use libsecp256k1 and it's python wrapper secp256k1-py
issue_body:
Currently there are two implementations of ecdsa in *pycoin*. A python one and an OpenSSL wrapper. The python one is quite slow, and I'm also a bit worried by the roll-your-own-crypto aspect of it (I'd rather see well-tested). The OpenSSL one is fast, but pulls in a rather large dependency and has some issues for me ([1][i1], [2][i2], [3][i]).

[i1]: https://github.com/richardkiss/pycoin/issues/176
[i2]: https://bugs.alpinelinux.org/issues/5264
[i3]: https://github.com/docker-library/python/issues/111

The other cryptocurrency libraries I'm using in my project pull in [*secp256k1*][b1] as a dependency. I suspect this library would be sufficient, fast and simplify the *pycoin* codebase.

[b1]: https://github.com/ludbb/secp256k1-py

I can work on a pull request if there is a chance it will be merged.

issue_title: Support path parameters (binarypath, dbpath, logpath) containing spaces
issue_body:
`mlaunch` does not currently support Windows paths that contain spaces. The only way to get it to work is to ensure that the binary path, db path and log path do not contain any spaces.

We [split up the command line using space as the delimiter](https://github.com/rueckstiess/mtools/blob/develop/mtools/mlaunch/mlaunch.py#L1607), and do not take double quotes into account.
 
### Expected behavior
Binary path, db path and log path should all be able to contain spaces.

### Actual/current behavior
Presently what happens is that mlaunch fails with the following output:
```
PS D:\development\mtools> mlaunch init --sharded 3 --replicaset --config 3 --mongos 2 --binarypath="C:\program files\mongodb\server\3.2.10-Enterprise"
The system cannot find the file C:\program.
None

can't start process, return code 1. tried to launch: start /b C:\program files\mongodb\server\3.2.10-Enterprise\mongod --replSet shard01 --dbpath D:\\development\\mtools\\data\\shard01/rs1\\db --logpath D:\\development\\mtools\\data\\shard01/rs1\\mongod.log --port 27019  --shardsvr --wiredTigerCacheSizeGB 1
```

### Steps to reproduce the actual/current behavior
Use a `mongod` binary that exists in a directory that contains a space, such as `C:\program files\mongodb\server\3.2.10-Enterprise`.

### Environment
Windows Server 2016

issue_title: Yesno default not intuitive
issue_body:
Taken from https://github.com/tylerdave/prompter/blob/master/prompter/__init__.py#L98
``` python
while True:
        response = get_input(prompt_text).strip()
        if response == '':
            return True
```

It seems to me that True is returned on an empty response, regardless of how the default parameter is set. Is this a bug, or have I completely missed something here?

Cheers!

Edit: After a little additional testing, I've found what I misunderstood.
Apparently the values True and False are always the same, but depending on if the default is Yes or No, they flip meaning, as they always mean "Was the default selected?".

I would argue that it would be more sensible to have "True" mean "Yes" and "False" mean "No" in all cases. How do you feel about this?

issue_title: support NLP
Generic Adaptor Support
issue_body:
Creating new issue to continue discussion.  From https://github.com/JimmXinu/FanFicFare/issues/223#issuecomment-335029787
{quote}
The first is proving that it's real-world useful with FFF. For that, I'd like to see working configurations for either some of the requested sites, some existing supported sites, or both. If it can't be configured to support say 3-4 known sites, I have to question how many other sites it would be useful for. Also keep in mind that because FFF runs as a Google web service, python pip CLI and a Calibre plugin, including non-standard libraries can be problematic--we already have to bundle several libraries in with FFF.

The second is that the existing FFF architecture is pretty firmly site==adapter==adapter source file. We have more than one site using the same code in places, but we generally do it by class inheritance. For proof-of-concept and initial testing, that's fine: add a new base_whatever_adapter.py file, add adapter_sitename.py files for each configured site, add the parser control configurations to defaults.ini. If a generic/configable adapter proves to be workable, and sufficiently easy for users to do, I'll need to then extend the architecture to allow sites to be added without adapter_sitename.py files.
{quote}

Apologies, as I'm new to FFF, I'm thinking I'll start small.  I will probably try to finish off the LNMTL adapter.  (As I've implemented this previously https://github.com/dteviot/WebToEpub/blob/master/plugin/js/parsers/LnmtlParser.js)

However, I had one other thought.  I also started out with a similar initial concept "site == adapter". However, I found a lot of sites were based on Wordpress or Blogspot,  So, the adapter selection engine rules became.:
1. Check the site name.  If known, use adapter X.
2. Check the content of the site.  If the DOM is Wordpress or Blogspot, use the Wordpress/Blogspot adapter.  (This worked by each adapter being able to register a "let me examine the DOM to see how confident I am I can handle it" function it with the adapter selection logic.  Which is currently only used by the Wordpress and Blogspot adapters.)
3. Try the "Generic Adapter", see if user has provided rules for site.  If not, ask user for config.

(In my code, what FFF calls Adapters, I call Parsers.  I've changed the wording to match the FFF terminology.)

issue_title: Mix of public and private suffixes
issue_body:
As the public suffix list is used to find the "TLD" (which is not the TLD in many cases), also private domains are now returned as TLD. Have a look at the list: https://publicsuffix.org/list/effective_tld_names.dat or https://publicsuffix.org/list/public_suffix_list.dat.

It is split into two sections, the public and the private suffixes. In the readme there is perfect example about how this get's wrong. The private suffixes can be submitted by every domain owner.

Eg. `co.uk` is a public suffix. A private suffix is e.g. `appspot.com`.

I see two issues here:
1. what you consider to be a "tld" is a (public or private) suffix plus the next domain name below. But that is not the "top level domain", it is first or second level (below the tld)! I think that deviation from standards should be documented *much* better.
2. you mix private and publix suffixes. So you treat e.g. `appspot.com` the same as `com` as equally valid suffixes! Have a look at the private suffix section in the list to see how much private domains are there.

issue_title: sections retrieved with the wiki API have different formating than content sections
issue_body:
The module is able to retrieve the table of content from the wikipedia API. A very basic functionality of this module is query the table of content and go over the sections. For example: 

```
import wikipedia

wikipedia.set_lang("nl")
page = wikipedia.page("Samenstelling_Tweede_Kamer_1875-1879")

secs = page.sections         #Get a complete list of the page's sections
print(page.section(secs[1])) 
print(page.section(secs[2])) #empty!
 ```

In this example the second section appears to be empty. This is due to the difference in data formatting of `wikipedia.sections` and `wiki.section`. 

```
https://nl.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?action=parse&prop=sections&page=Samenstelling_Tweede_Kamer_1875-1879&format=json
https://nl.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?explaintext=&rvprop=ids&titles=Samenstelling_Tweede_Kamer_1875-1879&prop=extracts|revisions&action=query&format=json
```
These are the actual queries made, the first one returns the sections in their plane formatting and the second returns the page content in wiki formatting. This causes the names retrieved by `wikipedia.sections` to differ from the section names in the `wikipedia.content`-content.

This is a problem I'm running into with my project; I'd like to access quotes for a specific person, and I'm missing some good way to access the WikiQuote API from Python, save wrapping it myself. Would you be open to a pull request that allows the user to override the API endpoint to access other Wikimedia sites?

issue_title: db.create_all() in LiveServerTestCase
issue_body:
In *Testing with SQLAlchemy* section of the document:

```
def setUp(self):
        db.create_all()
```
will cause error `RuntimeError: application not registered on db instance and no application bound to current context` with class `LiveServerTestCase`


which can be fixed by:
```
def setUp(self):
    with self.app.app_context():
        db.create_all()
```

issue_title: Continuation characters display error on Bash 3.x
issue_body:
An error is printed in Bash 3.x when a completion is returned that ends in a continuation character. [This test](https://github.com/kislyuk/argcomplete/blob/a433642df8aaac49e24be1fbda640f5777acd03e/test/test.py#L849) fails as a result.

The source of this problem is that compopt is not available in 3.x. The options are:
* Suppress the error (this won't stop the space being inserted)
* Revert #159 
* Add another option when registering, e.g. `--shell bash3` that restores the behavior before #159

issue_title: add validator argument
issue_body:
Example: we want to generate names, but names cannot start with numbers.

```python
from fauxfactory import gen_string

name = gen_string(
    'alpha', 
    validator=lambda x: not x.startswith(('1', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7', '8', '9', '0')),
    default='foobar',
    tries=10
)

or simpler

name = gen_string(
    'alpha', 
    validator=lambda x: not x[0].isdigit(),
    default='foobar',
    tries=10
)
```

So fauxfactory should accept `validator` which should return True, otherwise the value is `None` or `default`. If `tries` is set, then it retries to generate 10 times, if `tries` = `0` it tries to generate until validator is met!

BONUS: the validator must be callable or `re.Pattern` instance, so it checks for regex matching.

issue_title: SSLv3 Error When Using pyvmomi 6.0
issue_body:
There is an error when using pyvmomi 6.0 surrounding this code in core.py.

`        if not ssl_context:
            ctx = ssl.SSLContext(ssl.PROTOCOL_TLSv1)
            ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE
            self.ssl_context = ctx
        else:
            self.ssl_context = ssl_context`

I tested this on Python 2.7.6 and found an error here.  In order to fix it, I had to downgrade pyvmomi to 5.5 and remove the context parameter from the SmartConnect that is called from vconnector.

Did some research, and have not had a chance to fully test this, but this appears to be the fix:

`try:
    _create_unverified_https_context = ssl._create_unverified_context
except AttributeError:
    # Legacy Python that doesn't verify HTTPS certificates by default
    pass
else:
    # Handle target environment that doesn't support HTTPS verification
    ssl._create_default_https_context = _create_unverified_https_context`

This may require some adjustment for use with vconnector, so I have not reverted back to 6.0 pyvmomi to test as I have some automation that requires completion first.

Figured I would let you know via issue form.

	prompt "CHOICE"

config SYMBOL1
	default n
	bool "SYMBOL1"

config SYMBOL2

endchoice

SYMBOL1 and SYMBOL2 are dependent on CHOICE, so it will be very useful to have possibility to get this dependency by the API method or just from deserializied string option info (like "Additional dependencies from enclosing menus and ifs:" section).

Is it hard feature to implement? Thank you.

issue_title: Running several tests simultaneously on the same machine can lead to errors
issue_body:
For example, let's have the following sequence of statements executed in the given order in two different processes:

Process 1: device = uiautomator.Device(serial="1")
Process 2: device = uiautomator.Device(serial="2")
Process 1: device.screen.on()
Process 2: device.screen.on()

After this both processes will be attached to the same device "2", because local_port calculated in AutomatorServer.__init__() is not used until AutomatorServer.start(), and there is no way for process 2 to know that the port is already used when it selects it.

In addition adb forward will silently rebind port by default without returning an error, so neither of the processes will know that it talks to incorrect device.

issue_title: Allow easy extension of a Schema
issue_body:
Excuse me if this is somehow possible already, but consider the following code:

```
>>> base = Schema({'a': int})                                                                                          
>>> base.schema
{'a': <type 'int'>}
>>> tmp = base.schema.copy(); tmp.update({'b': str})
>>> extended = Schema(tmp)
>>> extended.schema
{'a': <type 'int'>, 'b': <type 'str'>}
```

But extending a Schema this way is really awful, partially due to python [not having a proper way to merge dicts](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/38987/how-can-i-merge-two-python-dictionaries-in-a-single-expression).

I would propose a syntax like this:

```
>>> base = Schema({'a': int}, required=True)
>>> extended = base.extend({'b': str})
>>> base.schema # original is kept intact
{'a': <type 'int'>}
>>> extended.schema
{'a': <type 'int'>, 'b': <type 'str'>}
>>> extended.required # inherits the required attribute as well
True
```

What are your thoughts on this? I need this to use voluptuous, so I could perhaps work on a PR.

issue_title: Using ANSI functions in PlatformManagerWindows.py
issue_body:
Hi, 
Thanks for the great work of migrating sikuli to pure python. I found that there are random crash (access violation) in:
    hdc = self._gdi32.CreateDCA(ctypes.c_char_p(device_name.encode("utf-8")), 0, 0, 0) 
on a x64 system. It looks like a issue of using ANSI function instead of using Unicode ones, as the address is some format like 0x00007fff00000000, and the behavior disappeared after I inserted a line of print device_name. 

I tried to fix it by changing them to Unicode format as:
-        self._gdi32.CreateDCA.restype = ctypes.c_void_p
 -        hdc = self._gdi32.CreateDCA(ctypes.c_char_p(device_name.encode("utf-8")), 0, 0, 0) # Convert to bytestring for c_char_p type
 +        self._gdi32.CreateDCW.restype = ctypes.c_void_p
 +        hdc = self._gdi32.CreateDCW(ctypes.c_wchar_p(unicode(device_name)), 0, 0, 0) # Convert to bytestring for c_wchar_p type

I will test this for a while and see if this really fixed my issue.

issue_title: MongoDBInstance hangs

I suspect the issue is the use of `subprocess.PIPE`, which has a limited buffer and will cause the subprocess to block if that buffer isn't consumed.

issue_title: (windows only?) message sent right before interpreter exit is never received
issue_body:
Hi,

With an ActiveMQ broker running on a machine called `broker` (not sure if it matters what kind of broker is running) and the following listener (`test_listener.py`) running:

```
stompClient = stomp.Connection([("broker", 61613)])
class MyListener(stomp.ConnectionListener):
   def on_error(self, headers, message):
       print('received an error "%s"' % message)
   def on_message(self, headers, message):
       print('received a message "%s"' % message)
stompClient.set_listener('', MyListener())
stompClient.start()
stompClient.connect()
stompClient.subscribe(destination='/topic/test_autoexit', id=1, ack='auto')

import time

while True:
    time.sleep(0.2)
```

When the following script (`test_sender.py`), is run on Windows (server 2008 R2 Enterprise SP1 with ActiveState python 2.7.1) the message it sends is never received by the listener. It works fine on linux (Ubuntu 14.04.3 LTS, python 2.7.6). 

```
import stomp
import time

stompClient = stomp.Connection([("broker", 61613)])
stompClient.start()
stompClient.connect()

stompClient.send(body="foo", destination="/topic/test_autoexit")
#print("about to sleep")
#time.sleep(1)
```

If I comment out the last two lines (thereby sleeping for one second after sending) and run it again, the message is received by the listener. The results are consistent.
So it has something to do (as far as I can tell) with exiting the interpreter right after calling `send()`.

Thanks.

issue_title: turn on TCP keepalive option, send ping every 10 minutes
issue_body:
I started a `wormhole send` last night, and didn't do the matching receive until the next morning. The two sides then failed to connect. It looks like both the sender and the server think they have a websocket connection open, but my home router's NAT table had dropped the forwarding entry because it hadn't seen any traffic in a while.

We need to turn on the `TCP_KEEPALIVE` option on both ends, so they eventually give up on connections that are broken this way. Note that `TCP_KEEPALIVE` doesn't actually keep anything alive, usually: it merely accelerates connection death. The specific behavior (using the default linux kernel settings is): when two hours pass without any outbound (ACK-able) traffic, start sending pings (which should provoke ACKs) every 75 seconds; when 11 of these have been sent without a response (about 10 minutes), drop the connection. The new messages occur far to late to prevent a NAT box from timing out. So it really just reduces the idle-until-dead time from infinity seconds to about two hours.

It's possible to change these settings in the kernel, but almost nobody does, so you always need some application-level timers.

We also need something to send a periodic ping message, maybe once every 5 or 10 minutes, to keep the NAT entry alive. I'll check to see if Autobahn already has an option for this.. seems like a pretty common issue with long-running websockets (something I'd expect to be included in the spec, in fact).

There's a pathological failure mode here, where the server is doing `TCP_KEEPALIVE`, but the client is not, and the NAT entry gets deleted. The server sees the socket disconnect (from the timeout), then eventually expires the nameplate (about 20min with the new expiration code). If the second client tries their `wormhole receive` after this point, they'll wind up re-allocating the same nameplate (since it's now idle), and then they'll wait forever for a sender which has already been lost. And the *sender* doesn't realize anything's gone wrong because they have keepalives turned off, so they think they still have a connection.

Fortunately, sending a periodic ping should both prevent the NAT entry from expiring, and will detect it fairly quickly (a few minutes, I think) if it ever does get dropped.

issue_title: minor race in exists check
issue_body:
When receiving to a file, wormhole checks if the file exists and refuses to overwrite it. This is a useful check, and kind of a security guard. However, there's a race condition in this check: The file may not exist when _decide_destname checks it, but could be created immediately afterwards. Then, wormhole would overwrite the newly created file with the file it receives.

This race seems unlikely to be usefully exploitable, but it is a chink in the defenses and such minor holes can sometimes prove unexpectedly fatal. (Perhaps if there was some way for the attacker to delay the wormhole receive so it does not open the file until a carefully chosen time...)

I suggest opening the file with O_CREATE | O_EXCL which will fail atomically if it already exists. Or, since it seems to rename received files into place from a temp file, renameat2() with RENAME_NOREPLACE may be useful.

issue_title: The sample code in the readme makes Sendgrid send back a 400.
Make OCR queue count visible?
issue_body:
When uploading stuff that needs OCR, sometimes we get backlogged and wish that clients would rate-limit. Last time this came up, hank suggested fetching this:

https://catalogd.archive.org/report/ocr.php?ocr_count=1

and stopping uploading if the count was > 400. Perhaps we could make this visible via ia? We'd want to normalize it by dividing by 400, so that we could update 400 to a different number in the future.

There might be some other upload-or-derive stats worth making visible. I guess S3 stress is already visible via 503s?

issue_title: Add more benchmark functions
issue_body:
Since this library is intended for researchers, I think it would be fitting to have a large range of benchmark functions at hand so one does not have to implement them himself. Good sources for such functions are:

* [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Test_functions_for_optimization) with a solid collection of benchmark functions and a good overview.
* [Benchmarkfcns](http://benchmarkfcns.xyz/fcns), they also have a quite big collection and even classification into different function categories (might be helpful for the documentation). They have fancy pictures and a short description for every benchmark function, as well as a MATLAB implementation.
* [Test Functions Index](http://infinity77.net/global_optimization/test_functions.html) with a ridiculous amount of benchmark functions. They even classified them by their hardness.

The functions can be implemented in the [`single_obj.py`](https://github.com/ljvmiranda921/pyswarms/blob/master/pyswarms/utils/functions/single_obj.py) file.
I think a good number to start with is 5 new benchmark functions.

Notes: Please work on the development branch. You can find a good StackOverflow question [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38564473/how-to-fork-a-branch-and-not-the-repository).

issue_title: Unable to use byte type parameters with Python 3
issue_body:
Hi,

Whenever I use a `byte` format field with `pyswagger` and Python 3 and pass in a Python `bytes` object, I hit an exception like the one below.

Looking at the code to convert the bytes to JSON [here](https://github.com/mission-liao/pyswagger/blob/c033e20d6c15492e3c3af5ebab13c944a7209a24/pyswagger/primitives/_byte.py#L25-L29), this actually returns `bytes` and not a `str`, matching the docs [here](https://docs.python.org/3/library/base64.html#base64.urlsafe_b64encode):

```
>>> import base64
>>> base64.urlsafe_b64encode(b'abcdef')
b'YWJjZGVm'
```

And passing that to the `JSONEncoder` causes a `TypeError` as below.

```
 File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\site-packages\pyswagger\contrib\client\requests.py", line 40, in request
    req.prepare(scheme=self.prepare_schemes(req), handle_files=False)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\site-packages\pyswagger\io.py", line 217, in prepare
    content_type, self.__data = self._prepare_body()
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\site-packages\pyswagger\io.py", line 83, in _prepare_body
    return content_type, self.__op._mime_codec.marshal(content_type, body, _type=_type, _format=_format, name=name)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\site-packages\pyswagger\primitives\codec.py", line 29, in marshal
    return codec.marshal(value, **kwargs)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\site-packages\pyswagger\primitives\codec.py", line 48, in marshal
    return json.dumps(value, cls=PrimJSONEncoder)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\json\__init__.py", line 237, in dumps
    **kw).encode(obj)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\json\encoder.py", line 199, in encode
    chunks = self.iterencode(o, _one_shot=True)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\json\encoder.py", line 257, in iterencode
    return _iterencode(o, 0)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\site-packages\pyswagger\primitives\comm.py", line 12, in default
    return json.JSONEncoder.default(self, obj)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python 3.5\lib\json\encoder.py", line 180, in default
    raise TypeError(repr(o) + " is not JSON serializable")
TypeError: b'AA==' is not JSON serializable
```

I think `Byte.to_json()` should return a `str` - I'll try and create a pull request with a fix.

Thanks!

issue_title: Bootstrap container plugin does not show in list of plugins that can be added to placeholder
issue_body:
Using `django-cms==3.3.2` and `djangocms-cascade==0.10.1` `BootstrapContainerPlugin` among others, does not show in Add plugin list.

This issue can be traced to these two recent changes:

* in djangocms-cascade `BootstrapContainerPlugin` got its `BootstrapJumbotronPlugin` as parent class here https://github.com/jrief/djangocms-cascade/blob/master/cmsplugin_cascade/bootstrap3/container.py#L42 and `SegmentPlugin` and `SimpleWrapperPlugin` here https://github.com/jrief/djangocms-cascade/blob/master/cmsplugin_cascade/plugin_base.py#L120 since they inherit from `TransparentMixin`

* in django cms there is this line https://github.com/divio/django-cms/blob/3.3.2/cms/plugin_pool.py#L196 where plugin is filtered out of the plugin list since it has set parent class which is checked here https://github.com/divio/django-cms/blob/3.3.2/cms/plugin_base.py#L177

Result is that Bootstrap container is not in the list of plugins that can be added to placeholder.

issue_title: Date issue occurring again - Cannot pass in a date earlier than one week ago
Potential Extension
issue_body:
I've been using markdown2 a lot in a recent project to convert a \LaTeX document generator into an online research environment.  Combined with MathJax, Markdown2 with the footnotes extension is  pretty much perfect.  However, the one thing that Markdown can't do is number figures and tables.  This is pretty much essential in research papers and is also a useful feature in other styles of documentation.  

Whilst I understand that there are way too many "standards" for Markdown, I wanted to add an extension to handle figure, table and equation numbering to Markdown.  What I've done (in a relatively few lines of code) is add a preprocessor which generates generic "counters" which can be used to number equations.  The syntax is as follows.

When you first want to label an item (figure, table, image whatever), you add a markdown item 

```
     [#counter_id](:item_tag)
```

and when you want to refer to it in the text you use `[:item_tag].  Here both `counter_id` and `item_tag` are "words" in the regex sense.

During conversion, the first pass identifies all the labels and creates counters as required and increments them if they're already created.  During the second pass, the counter integers are replaced in the text.  In principle, each counter can have it's own css style associated with it.  At present it doesn't hyperlink the references to the original item but that would be easy to add.

The code is about 40 lines of Python.

So the issue is: Is this more generally useful?  There's almost no support for "exhibit numbering" in any Markdown converters -- which is why I had to write my own.  I could certainly add this as an "extra" in markdown2 if there was general support for it but I don't want to add useless cruft to a project.  Comments?

issue_title: Race Condition results in "Incorrect authentication service"
issue_body:
I searched previous issues for "Incorrect authentication service" and it seems like there have been many, as there are a variety of problems that can occur resulting in the backend not being found.  Many of the issues are closed as the resulting problems were misconfigurations with backends, but I believe I've come across a fundamental problem that can occur with how backends are discovered. 

Note: It *won't* be a problem when using Django's development server as it's a single process/thread, but when running under a multi process/thread environment as most production environments will use, a race condition can sometimes occur.

The fundamental problem is that social_auth.backends.BACKENDS is populated at import time, and that may occur prior to all backend classes having actually been defined. Particularly, backends usually *require* importing base classes from social_auth.backends that they can subclass, prior to them having been defined. The BACKENDS population code then runs, and the backend classes aren't picked up as they haven't been defined yet.

Like I said you won't be able to reproduce this using Django's development server, but if you run a project using gunicorn you will eventually come across this issue after a number of restarts of gunicorn. Sometimes it will work, other times it won't. The behaviour basically sticks to each startup of a project, since the code only runs once when first imported.

A workaround for me has been to explicitly add my backend to social_auth.backends.BACKENDS in my urlconf and this resolves the issue. 

As a more permanent solution, I would recommend throwing out the whole approach of walking the filesystem from SOCIAL_AUTH_IMPORT_SOURCES, and just have SOCIAL_AUTH_ENABLED_BACKENDS defined as Python dotted paths (in the same way Django defines INSTALLED_APPS, MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES etc) that are imported inside get_backend, making get_backends, BACKENDS, and SOCIAL_AUTH_IMPORT_SOURCES no longer required. If this sounds like a good approach please let me know, I'd be happy to submit a pull request to fix it, but I thought I'd just gauge the direction you'd like to take first. 

Finally, thanks for the awesome app - it really is an amazing piece of work that obviously a ton of effort has been put into, and one of my always-go-to solutions for the problem it solves.

issue_title: Pip can't find the source for unicodecsv
issue_body:
When I try to install unicodecsv I get the following error: 
(rs3) chris@fen-desktop2(rs3)$ pip install -v unicodecsv
Downloading/unpacking unicodecsv
  Could not find any downloads that satisfy the requirement unicodecsv
No distributions at all found for unicodecsv
The log looks like this (abridged): 
Downloading/unpacking unicodecsv
  Getting page http://pypi.python.org/simple/unicodecsv
  URLs to search for versions for unicodecsv:
  * http://pypi.python.org/simple/unicodecsv/
  Getting page https://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv
  Analyzing links from page http://pypi.python.org/simple/unicodecsv/
    Skipping link https://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv (from http://pypi.python.org/simple/unicodecsv/); not a file
  Analyzing links from page https://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv
    Skipping link https://github.com/opensearch.xml (from https://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv); unknown archive format: .xml
...

    Skipping link git://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv.git (from https://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv); unknown archive format: .git
...
  Could not find any downloads that satisfy the requirement unicodecsv
No distributions at all found for unicodecsv
The problem seems to be that there are no links to .tar.gz files on that page (the project home page), and pip doesn't like to install from git without further configuration. I think if you added either: * a link to the downloads page (https://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv/archives/master), or * a specially-formatted git URL to the PyPi page at http://pypi.python.org/simple/unicodecsv/ that might be enough to enable pip to find and install the package? As a workaround for anyone who needs to put this in their requirements.txt, you can add the following line instead: 
git+git://github.com/jdunck/python-unicodecsv.git
Cheers, Chris.

issue_title: Setting LEVEL_2 value for covers such as HmIP-BBL
issue_body:
I am running Home Assistant but i guess the problem has nothing to do with Home Assistant.

**Description of problem:**
I am using the HmIP-BBL Jalousies, which have 2 LEVELS one (LEVEL) for the actual level of the cover and the other one (LEVEL_2) for the slats position (Das ist der Neigungswinkel der Jalousie). 

The following python script reads the LEVEL_2 succesfully from the device, but setting LEVEL_2 does not have an effect:

```Python
import time
def syscb(src, *args):
    print(src)
    for arg in args:
        print(arg)

device_address = '001MYADDRESS51'
my_ip = "192.168.1.255"

from pyhomematic import HMConnection

pyhomematic = HMConnection(local=my_ip, localport=7080, remote="192.168.1.254", remoteport=2010, systemcallback=syscb)
pyhomematic.start()
time.sleep(0.2)

# directly getting channel 4 to set the value directly (i tried all the other ones just in case)
blind_channel = pyhomematic.devices_all['default'][device_address+':4']
level = blind_channel.getValue("LEVEL_2")
print(level)
success = blind_channel.setValue("LEVEL_2", 0.2)
print(success)
level = blind_channel.getValue("LEVEL_2")
print(level)
```
output from the above script is:
0.55
True
0.55


**Traceback or log (if applicable):**

Happy new Year
Steffen

Ps:
I have already implemented (more like copied and changed) a helper to use it directly in a device with the same effect. get_level_2 works, set_level_2 doesn't.

```Python
class HelperActorLevel2(HMDevice):
    """
    Generic shutter level functions
    """
    def __init__(self, device_description, proxy, resolveparamsets=False):
        super().__init__(device_description, proxy, resolveparamsets)

        # init metadata
        self.WRITENODE.update({"LEVEL_2": self.ELEMENT})

    def get_level_2(self, channel=None):
        """Return current level. Return value is float() from 0.0 to 1.0."""
        return self.getWriteData("LEVEL_2", channel)

    def set_level_2(self, position, channel=None):
        """Seek a specific value by specifying a float() from 0.0 to 1.0."""
        try:
            position = float(position)
        except Exception as err:
            LOG.debug("HelperActorLevel2.set_level_2: Exception %s" % (err,))
            return False

        self.writeNodeData("LEVEL_2", position, channel)
```

issue_title: Just importing setproctitle affected /proc/{pid}/comm
issue_body:
I've observed that `/proc/pid/comm` is changed during `spt_setup`, which is invoked in INIT_MODULE.
I think we'd better off leaving it unchanged until `setproctitle` or `getproctitle` is called.

issue_title: RFE: support Ubuntu
issue_body:
Travis CI environments are Ubuntu (containers) by default. It would be great to `pip install rpm-py-installer` in these environments directly.

Ubuntu has "rpm" available https://packages.ubuntu.com/source/bionic/rpm . However, when I `apt-get install rpm`, the rpmdb on Ubuntu contains no knowledge of any installed packages, so `rpm -q <anything>` will always fail. rpm-py-installer runs `rpm -q rpm-libs`, and this fails.

    reading manifest file 'pip-egg-info/rpm_py_installer.egg-info/SOURCES.txt'

issue_title: Travis builds failing due to removed OpenSSL symbol
issue_body:
All pull requests since 0.2.5 have returned a "failed" build status. Logs return as follows:

    /usr/bin/ld: .libs/snmpusm.o: undefined reference to symbol 'BN_bn2bin@@OPENSSL_1.0.0'
    //lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcrypto.so.1.0.0: error adding symbols: DSO missing from command line
    collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status

It would appear that this is also seen in [bug 2711](https://sourceforge.net/p/net-snmp/bugs/2711/) of Net-SNMP and fixed in commit [088622](https://sourceforge.net/p/net-snmp/code/ci/088622974007093fbb63f4a5964427f3c754e26d/).

A patch may need to be applied after the `cd net-snmp-$NET_SNMP_VERSION` command.

Once this is completed, the three most recent pull requests will need to be rebuilt before accepting them into the master branch

issue_title: byweekday param
issue_body:
Hi, it looks like you don't support the byweekday params. From the dateutil doc you can read:

_byweekday: [...]  It's also possible to use an argument n for the weekday instances, which will mean the nth occurrence of this weekday in the period. For example, with MONTHLY, or with YEARLY and BYMONTH, using FR(+1) in byweekday will specify the first friday of the month where the recurrence happens._

This means that I can use byweekday:1, but not byweekday:MO(1)

Some things can be done using the bysetpos param, but not all of them. Am I wrong? 

Why did you choose not to support it?

Thanks!

issue_title: lms.py compile failure
issue_body:
```python
import numpy as np

#pythran export lms (float[], int, float[], float[], float[], int, float)
def lms (A, M, y1, y2, y3, k, alpha):
    errors = np.empty (len(A)-M, dtype=complex)
    if alpha is None:
        alpha = np.zeros (M, dtype=complex)
    for i in range (M//2, len(A)-M//2-1):
        z = y1[i] - np.dot (alpha, y2[i-(M//2):i+M//2+1])

        alpha += k * z * y3[i-(M//2):i+M//2+1].conj()
        errors[i-M//2] = (z - A[i])
```

issue_title: Aalen additive fitter gives inf values; benchmark proposed
issue_body:
The Aalen additive model's predict() method in Lifelines often returns several inf values and an alternative is proposed. We begin with an example and then link to our proposed solution.

**As an example of encountered inf values:**
from lifelines import AalenAdditiveFitter
from lifelines.datasets import load_rossi
rossi_dataset = load_rossi()
aaf = AalenAdditiveFitter()
train = rossi_dataset.head(300)
test = rossi_dataset.tail(100)
test['pred'] = aaf.predict(test)
test['pred'].describe()
test['pred'].describe()['75%']
inf

**Potential alternative:**
https://github.com/IvanBarrientos/Survival-Analysis/blob/master/aalen_additive_oop.py

Example that alternative gives finite predictions and seems to work
from aalen_additive_oop import AalenAdditveModel
train = rossi_dataset.head(300)
test = rossi_dataset.tail(100)
aam = AalenAdditiveModel()
features = list(train)[2:]
aam.fit(train, features, 'week', 'arrest')
preds = [aam.predict(test[features].ix[k]) for k in test.index]  # preds are based on trapz, not medians
max(preds)
1246.6497646919993  # note that we do not get inf values
min(preds)
8.8548376660620853  # the predictions in this example seem reasonable

issue_title: Publish Scala 2.11 Builds to Maven Repo
issue_body:
Seems like it would be consistent with spark-core to publish both 2.10 and 2.11 builds.

Obviously this is not a very high priority, as one can just build their own.   We do :)

As far as I can tell, this fork of python-gnupg is also vulnerable. However, I couldn't test it, because it didn't work with my version of GnuPG.
In any case, this is a heads up. You might consider adding `--no-verbose` to the cmdline.

issue_title: Why so many f*cking commits!?
issue_body:
If it seems like this package changes on a daily basis, you're not far off.

The reason is that there are some features that were not quite complete and there are some changes that can really benefit users.

For example, in the upcoming 2.6 release, the automatic sizing of buttons is no longer controlled by the automatic text sizing setting.  Buttons have their own settings.  This potentially removes a lot of extra source code that dealt with these text sizing issues.  Now you simply set your buttons to always resize and work with the text resizing on a one-off by one-off case.

As long as there are new features, like colored forms, going into the code, as well as additional settings that users can immediately benefit from, then there will be releases up to PyPI on a regular basis.

issue_title: `get_subscriber_hash()` should not call `str.encode()`
issue_body:
I ran into an error today in my app that uses `mailchimp3`, and tracked it down to the `get_subscriber_hash()` function calling `member_email.lower().encode()`. This use of `encode()` is problematic for three reasons:

1) Leaving out the _encoding_ argument causes `encode()` to use the default encoding, which is probably ascii. But it might be something else, and you definitely dont want this function to perform differently on different systems.
2) Encoding the email address to ascii will throw an exception for perfectly legal email addresses. in the "Note" section on [this page](http://kb.mailchimp.com/accounts/management/international-characters-in-mailchimp) of the MailChimp docs shows that MailChimp accepts email addresses with non-ascii characters in the domain name.
3) If `member_email` is a raw python string, rather than a Unicode string, calling `encode()` on it for this purpose is pointless and potentially error-prone (this is only likely in Python 2, since strings are Unicode by default in PY3). A raw python string is, by definition, already encoded. So unless you want to re-encode it to something completely different, like base64, calling `encode()` has no effect except to throw an exception for potentially valid addresses.

If I knew a really "appropriate" solution to this, I'd have made this a PR instead of an issue. My best guess for a good solution would be something like this:

    def get_subscriber_hash(member_email):
        """
        The MD5 hash of the lowercase version of the list member's email.
        Used as subscriber_hash
    
        :param member_email: The member's email address
        :type member_email: :py:class:`str`
        :returns: The MD5 hash in hex
        :rtype: :py:class:`str`
        """
        check_email(member_email)
        member_email = member_email.lower().encode('utf8')
        m = hashlib.md5(member_email)
        return m.hexdigest()

The addition of the `'utf8'` argument solves issues 1 and 2, but doesn't really solve 3. I'm not entirely certain if that's a problem, though, since the most likely encoding for a non-Unicode string is ascii. An ascii string is already valid utf8, so the `encode('utf8')` call should do nothing and not throw an exception. But if it's, say, a `cp1251`-encoded (windows encoding) string, I don't know what `encode('utf8')` will do.
