pycparser==2.18
pyethash==0.1.27
pyflakes==1.6.0
pysha3==1.0.2
pytest==3.1.2
pytest-asyncio==0.8.0
pytest-cov==2.5.1
pytest-logging==2015.11.4
pytest-xdist==1.18.1
rlp==0.4.7
six==1.11.0
toolz==0.9.0
tox==2.7.0
trie==1.1.0
typing==3.6.2
virtualenv==15.1.0
yarl==1.1.0
```

### What is wrong?

For some reason I don't understand, there's an error in the constants imports in [evm/chains/mainnet/__init__.py](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/evm/chains/mainnet/__init__.py). It appeared with [this commit](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/e1515b6b196a7fbdeb97657101a6eed62c1a1435) due to [this change](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/64328ecd60a8f50e3ab706c254efd30d6fec3363#diff-2c154d088c2a7f106917603a2fb872a4) to `evm/chains/mainnet/constants.py`.

You can recreate the error as follows.

Check out [this commit](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/64328ecd60a8f50e3ab706c254efd30d6fec3363#diff-2c154d088c2a7f106917603a2fb872a4) and run:

```
> python -c 'from evm import constants
```

It runs without a problem.

Then check out [this commit](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/b6f251956105d97270afca462e1df98b2a6f4b7f) (which just changes the orders of the constants imports back to what they were before the fix) and you'll get an error:

```
> python -c 'from evm import constants'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "evm/__init__.py", line 13, in <module>
    from evm.chains import (  # noqa: F401
  File "evm/chains/__init__.py", line 6, in <module>
    from .mainnet import (  # noqa: F401
  File "evm/chains/mainnet/__init__.py", line 47, in <module>
    mix_hash=constants.ZERO_HASH32,
AttributeError: module 'evm.chains.mainnet.constants' has no attribute 'ZERO_HASH32'
```

The culprit appears to be [these imports](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/evm/chains/mainnet/__init__.py#L3-L9). For some reason, `from .constants import...` seems to define a module global `constants` pointing to the chain constants file.

### How can it be fixed

Swapping the order of these imports (as [here](https://github.com/lrettig/py-evm/commit/b6f251956105d97270afca462e1df98b2a6f4b7f)) makes the error go away but we should understand what's causing it in the first place.

issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently, the closest that we have to a CLI is a number of the python modules are directly executable to make testing and development easier.

We will need a real CLI at some point soon.

### Things to figure out

- What we use for the CLI interface
  - [`click`](http://click.pocoo.org/)
  - [`argparse`](https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/argparse.html) from the standard library
- What do we expose over the CLI
  - We can probably borrow heavily from `geth/parity`
  - Short list:
    - run the node (mainnet/ropsten)
    - drop into python console
    - JSON-RPC server

* OS: linux
* Python Version (python --version): 3.6.3

### What is wrong?

Gas estimation should not require a signed transaction.  An example use case is getting a gas price estimate for transactions before local signing, where the private key is not available to the node.

### How can it be fixed

The instance of `SpoofTransaction` can be extended to provide the rest of the missing attributes from an unsigned transaction object.  The following stack trace + exceptions show each dependent attribute that would need to be added to SpoofTransaction when passing an unsigned transaction object.     minimum_transaction = SpoofTransaction(transaction, gas=transaction.intrinsic_gas)

* Python Version: 3.6.4rc1 (shown when running `python --version`)
* OS: osx - macOS 10.12.6


### What was wrong?

I used PyEnv to install and switch to the latest Python version 3.6.4rc1 https://github.com/pyenv/pyenv. I then installed the following Python packages:
* https://pypi.python.org/pypi/py-solc/2.1.0

python scripts/main.py

I then created and executed a Python file i.e. main.py with `python main.py`:
**main.py**

import solc
from solc import compile_files

I'm trying to replicate using Web3.py and py-solc the same functionality that worked when I used Web3.js and solc-js here: https://github.com/ltfschoen/geth-node/blob/master/scripts/main.js

In the file I use py-solc to compile Solidity source code from a file using the function `compile_files`, but I get a full output error in Bash terminal as follows:

issue_title: Getting TDL cons- and diff-lists
issue_body:
Currently, getting the items from a list in TDL is rather inconvenient. E.g., for the orthography tokens from a lexical entry: `entry['ORTH.FIRST']`, `entry['ORTH.REST.FIRST']`, etc.

It would be more convenient to have a function to extract these to Pythonic lists. Here's such a function for cons lists:

```python
def extract_cons_list(d):
    if d is None: return []
    items = [d['FIRST']]
    items.extend(extract_cons_list(d['REST']))
    return items
```

issue_title: Question: Why does the `pkgs_dirs` variable not behave as expected?
issue_body:
<!--
Hi!  Read this; it's important.

This is an issue tracker for conda -- the package manager.  File feature requests
for conda here, as well as bug reports about something conda has messed up.

If your issue is a feature request for:
  * a specific conda package from Anaconda ('defaults' channel):
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * a specific conda package from conda-forge:
      ==> file at the corresponding feedstock under https://github.com/conda-forge
  * repo.anaconda.com access and service:
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * anaconda.org access and service:
      ==> file at https://anaconda.org/contact/report
  * commands under 'conda build':
      ==> file at https://github.com/conda/conda-build
  * commands under 'conda env':
      ==> please file it here!
  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

-->

Here is my `.condarc` file (there are copies both in my home/user directory, as well as in the root installation directory `/opt/miniconda`, and both have the same contents)

```
envs_dirs:
  - /opt/miniconda
pkgs_dirs:
  - /opt/miniconda/pkgs
```

Based on this, I would have expected, when creating a new Conda environment, that the files `urls` and `urls.txt` would be placed in `/opt/miniconda/pkgs`. Instead, they are placed in `/opt/pkgs`.

My expectation is based on my (evidently incorrect) reading of [the relevant conda documentation](https://conda.io/docs/user-guide/configuration/use-condarc.html#specify-package-directories-pkgs-dirs).

I had to manually give my user write permission for `/opt/miniconda` and all objects contained therein. I thought this would have been enough to use Conda based on my `.condarc`, but instead it was also necessary to give my user write permission for `/opt/pkgs` and all objects contained therein.

Is there a way to change the Conda settings yet further so as to make necessary _only_ the directory `/opt/miniconda`? (And make unnecessary using `/opt/pkgs` when creating environments?)

issue_title: "conda update --file pkgs.txt" results in "Error: no package names supplied"
issue_body:
This is a weird result:

```bash
[root@silver ~]# /opt/wakari/anaconda/bin/conda update -p /opt/wakari/anaconda --file install.txt 
Error: no package names supplied
# If you want to update to a newer version of Anaconda, type:
#
# $ conda update --prefix /opt/wakari/anaconda anaconda

[root@silver ~]# cat install.txt 
bokeh=0.9.2=np19py27_0
```

I thought maybe it was because `install.txt` had version information, so I created `update.txt` without version info and tried again, but same result.

```bash
[root@silver ~]# /opt/wakari/anaconda/bin/conda update -p /opt/wakari/anaconda --file update.txt 
Error: no package names supplied
# If you want to update to a newer version of Anaconda, type:
#
# $ conda update --prefix /opt/wakari/anaconda anaconda

[root@silver ~]# cat update.txt 
bokeh
```

In contrast, doing the same thing but replacing `--file update.txt` with \` ` cat update.txt ` \` worked fine.

But AFAICT, the `--file` option doesn't let me do what the help says it should allow:

```
[root@silver ~]# /opt/wakari/anaconda/bin/conda update -h
...
optional arguments:
  --file FILE           Read package versions from FILE.
```

issue_title: Mixing and matching python 2 and python 3 environments with separate modules
issue_body:
I've been working with a python 3.6.0 installation set up with Ananconda 4.3.0:
```python
Python 3.6.0 |Anaconda 4.3.0 (64-bit)| (default, Dec 23 2016, 12:22:00) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-1)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
```
I'm collaborating on a project that is written in python 2.x and I'd like to use all the `conda` tools, so I created a new python 2.7 environment for the project following the guidelines [here](https://conda.io/docs/using/envs.html) and [here](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/30492623/using-both-python-2-x-and-python-3-x-in-ipython-notebook/30492913#30492913). Specifically:
```bash
conda create -n py27 python=2.7 ipykernel
source activate py27
```
I can confirm the environments are setup correctly:
```bash
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] 1 $ source activate py27
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] 2s $ python --version
Python 2.7.13 :: Continuum Analytics, Inc.
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] $ source activate root
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [root] 2s $ python --version
Python 3.6.0 :: Anaconda 4.3.0 (64-bit)
```

While in the `py27` environment, I run `conda install yaml` to install the `yaml` module for that environment and I'm told it's already installed (and the path looks right to me, showing `py27`):
```bash
# All requested packages already installed.
# packages in environment at /home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27:
#
yaml                      0.1.6                         0  
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] 4s $ 
```
But if I launch `python` in the `py27` environment, I can't load the module, even though the module is installed in (seemingly) the right place:
```python
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] 1 $ python -c "import sys; print sys.version; print sys.path; import yaml"
2.7.13 |Continuum Analytics, Inc.| (default, Dec 20 2016, 23:09:15) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-1)]
['', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python27.zip', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/plat-linux2', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/lib-tk', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/lib-old', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/lib-dynload', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/setuptools-27.2.0-py2.7.egg']
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: No module named yaml
```
It does work, however, if I call `python3` from within the `py27` environment and load `yaml`. It appears to me that the `yaml` module is installed in the `py27` environment, but only for python 3.x. Is there a way to force `conda` to install this module for python 2.x? Am I misunderstanding how the modules go along with the environments?  As far as I can tell, from running `conda list` in either environment, I have `yaml` version 0.1.6 installed, both with "build string" set to "0".

issue_title: ipython installation does not work properly in new environment
issue_body:
Hi,

I think there is a bug when ipython is installed within a new conda environment

I have python 3.4.3 in my root environment, now I want to create a new environment with python 2.7, so I execute

conda create -n test python=2.7
source activate test

which python    -> somepath/conda/envs/test/bin/python
which ipython   -> somepath/conda/bin/ipython


now, **I start ipython** and naturally it loads python 3.4.3 because its still using the ipython from the root installation, so I go

conda install ipython

which python    -> somepath/conda/envs/test/bin/python
which ipython   -> somepath/conda/envs/test/bin/ipython

when starting ipython now, it still loads the wrong python version (3.4.3. instead of 2.7). Note that this behaviour only occurs if the wrong ipython is started before the conda installation. So the problem can be avoided by installing ipython right from the start:

conda create -n test1 python=2.7 ipython

I don't understand why this happens but I though it might be worth mentioning it here.


Cheers!

issue_title: Package dependencies not resolved correctly
issue_body:
``pyqt=4.11.4``'s dependencies resolve to ``sip=4.16.9``:

```
$ conda install pyqt=4.11.4
Package plan for installation in environment C:\ANACONDA:
The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    pyqt: 4.11.4-py27_7 defaults
    sip:  4.16.9-py27_2 defaults
```

However, this version of ``sip`` is incompatible with ``pyqt=4.11.4`` (which breaks ``matplotlib``). I was about to report this in the PyQT bug tracker when I suddenly discovered that the ``pyqt`` package properly requires ``sip=4.18``:

 - download https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/win-64/pyqt-4.11.4-py27_7.tar.bz2
 - open up ``pyqt-4.11.4-py27_7.tar.bz2 ! info/index.json``:

```
{
  "arch": "x86_64", 
  "build": "py27_7", 
  "build_number": 7, 
  "depends": [
    "python 2.7*", 
    "qt 4.8.*", 
    "sip 4.18"
  ]
}
```

As you can see, ``pyqt`` requires ``sip 4.18``, not ``4.16``. I'm running ``conda=4.2.7``.

issue_title: Ipython with Python.app distributed with conda will not parse sys_version correctly.
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:

1. Install conda3
2. `conda install python.app ipython`
3. `pythonw -m IPython`

Will raise a ValueError (from _anaconda3/lib/python3.6/platform.py_) saying it failed to parse Cpython sys.version.
Here's the offending code:

```
    else:
        # CPython
        match = _sys_version_parser.match(sys_version)
        if match is None:
            raise ValueError(
                'failed to parse CPython sys.version: %s' %
                repr(sys_version))
```
That's because `pythonw`'s sys.version is:

> "'3.6.3 |Anaconda, Inc.| (default, Oct  6 2017, 12:04:38) \\n[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Clang 4.0.1 (tags/RELEASE_401/final)]'"

My workaround was to modify the match copying the sys_version of python3, as in:
```
    else:
        # CPython
        match = _sys_version_parser.match(sys_version) or _sys_version_parser.match('3.6.3 | packaged by conda-forge | (default, Oct  5 2017, 19:18:17) \n[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 6.1.0 (clang-602.0.53)]')
        if match is None:
            raise ValueError(
                'failed to parse CPython sys.version: %s' %
                repr(sys_version))
```

Don't know what is the correct place to report this, but I figured here.
Thanks!

issue_title: Copying default values from configspec changes encoding
issue_body:
When specifying an encoding for a config file with a configspec applying the configspec to copy the default values will change the encoding of the config file.

Here is the python snippet that shows the problem:

    from configobj import ConfigObj
    from validate import Validator

    configspec = '''
        version = float
    '''.splitlines()

    config = ConfigObj(configspec=configspec,
                       write_empty_values=True,
                       create_empty=True,
                       encoding='utf8')

    # verify that encoding is set correctly:
    print(config.encoding)  # will return 'utf8', correct


    # apply configspec while copying default values
    config.validate(Validator(), copy=True)

    # again verify that encoding is set correctly:
    print(config.encoding)  # will return 'None', incorrect

To get around this you have to create the configspec as a distinct ConfigObject object specifying its encoding and hand it over to the real ConfigObj.

issue_title: np.datetime64 not handled properly in fetchwhere
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm trying to use the fetchwhere function to filter a bcolz table I have, but am unable to do so when filtering a column of np.datetime64 type since it generates the following error: IndexError: invalid index to scalar variable.

Here's a small snippet of code that generates this error: 

```
import numpy as np
import bcolz

N = int(5)
ct = bcolz.fromiter(((i,i*i) for i in range(N)), dtype="i4,f8", count=N)
new_col = [np.datetime64('2018-03-01'), np.datetime64('2018-03-02'), np.datetime64('2018-03-03'), np.datetime64('2018-03-04'), np.datetime64('2018-03-05')]
ct.addcol(new_col)
threshold = np.datetime64('2018-03-03')
ct.fetchwhere('(f2 > threshold)', user_dict={'threshold': threshold})

```

I've attached a simple ipython notebook where I run this code and successfully filter columns with an int parameter, but fail to do so using a np.datetime64 parameter. I believe the issue lies in the _eval_blocks function in chunked_eval.py. Here, we do the following check:

```
if hasattr(var, "__getitem__"):
    vars_[name] = var[:]
```

Unfortunately, np.datetime64 objects satisfy that condition and have a __getitem__ attr, despite operating like scalar values. Could we perhaps use a different check than if hasattr(var, "__getitem__")? Maybe use something like np.isscalar()?

Thanks!
[bcolz_test.pdf](https://github.com/Blosc/bcolz/files/1858178/bcolz_test.pdf)

issue_title: Hardclipped bases in cigar ignored by pysam.AlignedSegment.infer_query_length()
issue_body:
The [ `pysam.AlignedSegment.infer_query_length`](http://pysam.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#pysam.AlignedSegment.infer_query_length) method ignores hardclipped bases.  The method states that it gets the:

> inferred read length from CIGAR string.

this is in contrast with the [`pysam.AlignedSegment.query_length `](http://pysam.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#pysam.AlignedSegment.query_length)

>This value corresponds to the length of the sequence supplied in the BAM/SAM file. The length of a query is 0 if there is no sequence in the BAM/SAM file. In those cases, the read length can be inferred  from the CIGAR alignment, see `pysam.AlignmentFile.infer_query_length()`.

With a cigar value of `58H150M` which represents an alignment where 58 bases have been hardclipped (not present in the alignment or SEQ section) and 150 matches. The SEQ section has 150 bases.  If `query_length=150` and `infer_query_length()=150`, however this should be: `infer_query_length()=208` by my understanding of the docs.  Am I missing something / is this intentional or just something to be corrected at some point?

Example of this can be found and run here: https://gist.github.com/amblina/795d30b5e04f87090d61739cb1846c1b 

Output:
```
@HD	VN:1.3	SO:unsorted
@SQ	SN:Test_reference	LN:200
query1	0	Test_reference	51	255	58H150M	*	0	0	TCTCCGACTGTTCCCAAATTGTAACTTATCGTTCCGTGAAGGCCAGAGTCACTTCCCGGCCCTTTCCATGCGCGCACCATACCCTCCTAGTTCCCCGGTTATCTTTCCGAAGTGGGAGTGAGCGAACCTCCGTTTACGTCTTGTTACCAA	*	NM:i:1	AS:i:148

Infered length of query: 150
SEQ field length: 150
CIGAR field: 58H150M, length: 208
Actual length of original query: 208
```

issue_title: Curator snapshot action timeout override
issue_body:
As discuses with @untergeek on [discuss.elastic.co](https://discuss.elastic.co/t/curator-snapshot-action-timeout-override/58362) automatic `timeout_override` for actions like **snapshot** and **forcemerge** is not working in latest version of curator. This should be mentioned somewhere ideally in documentation found [here](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/client/curator/current/configfile.html#timeout).

This is quote of @untergeek reply to my question on discuss.elastic.co relevant to this issue:

> You need to manually change the `timeout_override` for these actions for now. I initially had the feature in place, but removed it when I added `timeout_override`.
>But don't despair, the automatic, longer `timeout_override` for these actions will be fixed in 4.1.0 as a part of the schema validation feature being worked on right now.

issue_title: Schema errors, likely due to new version of voluptuous
issue_body:
### Expected Behavior
No errors when executing a rollover.

### Actual Behavior
```
2018-02-15 15:26:57,330 ERROR     Schema error: expected str for dictionary value @ data['new_index']
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/validators/schemacheck.py", line 57, in result
    return self.schema(self.config)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/voluptuous/schema_builder.py", line 267, in __call__
    return self._compiled([], data)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/voluptuous/schema_builder.py", line 587, in validate_dict
    return base_validate(path, iteritems(data), out)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/voluptuous/schema_builder.py", line 425, in validate_mapping
    raise er.MultipleInvalid(errors)
voluptuous.error.MultipleInvalid: expected str for dictionary value @ data['new_index']

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "venv/bin/curator", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(cli())
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/click/core.py", line 722, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/click/core.py", line 697, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/click/core.py", line 535, in invoke
    return callback(*args, **kwargs)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/cli.py", line 211, in cli
    run(config, action_file, dry_run)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/cli.py", line 119, in run
    action_dict = validate_actions(action_config)
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/utils.py", line 1418, in validate_actions
    loc
  File "venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/validators/schemacheck.py", line 68, in result
    self.test_what, self.location, self.badvalue, self.error)
curator.exceptions.ConfigurationError: Configuration: options: Location: Action ID "2", action "rollover": Bad Value: "(could not determine)", expected str for dictionary value @ data['new_index']. Check configuration file.
```

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
Run Curator with an action step configured like so:
```
  1:
    action: rollover
    description: Rollover my alias
    options:
      name: my_alias
      conditions:
        max_docs: 13000000  
```
### Specifications

  - Version: 5.4.1
  - Platform: Python 3.5.2 on Ubuntu 16.04


## Context (Environment)
The issue has halted our nightly execution of Curator to rollover some indices (among other tasks).

## Detailed Description
Curator v5.4.1 began throwing a new ConfigurationError for me last night despite no changes in our configuration. After some investigation, I think this may be related to the recent release of a new version of Voluptuous. We run Curator on a CI server, and the job rebuilds the virtualenv on each run. Since Curator doesn't explicitly pin the Voluptuous version, the behavior changed with the new release. I am not certain whether the fix ought to be in Curator or Voluptuous but figured it couldn't hurt to open here.

A potential solution might be to have `setup.py` explicitly pin the prior version of Voluptuous, but there's probably something better.

If I run the following (admitted contrived) example, in which I want to get the names of my VNETs:

`az network vnet show --ids $(az network vnet list -g {rg} --query [].id -o tsv) --query [].name`

I get the following:
```
[

issue_title: Improve/implement deprecation strategies for commands, command groups, options, parameters
issue_body:
Currently we have a couple strategies for deprecating things in the CLI:

**commands** - deprecation message
**parameters** - argparse.SUPPRESS allows them to not show up but still be usable.
**options** - no mechanism
**command groups** - no mechanism, though you can have deprecation messages for each command in the group

Ideally, there should be mechanism to deprecate any and all of these things that has the following properties:
1) displays a message if the deprecated thing is used
2) allows the deprecated thing to be hidden from help but still be usable
3) allows the dev to specify an expiration date, after which it is as though the thing didn't exist (using it will cause the regular error). A command table linter rule can be used to identify deprecated items which have passed their "expiration date" and thus can be removed.

---

### Environment summary

Install Method (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, edge build) / CLI version (`az --version`) / OS version / Shell Type (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  

```
azure-cli (2.0.30)
```

issue_title: [App Service] Allow configuring/detecting the current web app
issue_body:
Similar to the request in #1377, it would be awesome if a user could also configure a default/current web app instance, so that they could use all of the `az appservice web` commands, without needing to specify either a resource group or a web app.
 
This would help improve the overall efficiency of the CLI, for scenarios where a dev is working exclusively with a specific web app, and therefore, shouldn't need to be explicit about the context we could otherwise infer. Many ubiquitous CLIs provide this kind of behavior, to enable an increased ergonomic experience.

I could see this either being a new setting of the `az configure` command, or a config file that is placed within the app directory itself. That way, when you `cd` into a project directory, it can automatically know which web app you're dealing with. I'm not too opinionated about the exact solution to this, but I would very much love to see `az appservice web` commands be usable without needing to specify the `-n` flag at all.

issue_title: azure-mgmt-keyvault calls to KeyVaultManagementClient get_deleted and list_deleted fail due to SerializationError
issue_body:
datetime strings returned for DeletedVaultProperties are currently returned in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ'.  However this isn't ISO8601 complient which requires a T to separate the date and time so the format should be 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ'.  This causes python SDK clients and CLI clients to fail to deserialize the response from methods returning DeletedVaultProperties so all calls to get_deleted or list_deleted fail with exception.

```
Cannot deserialize datetime object., ValueError: Invalid datetime string: 2017-05-26 00:06:17Z
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "c:\src\azure-cli\env\lib\site-packages\msrest\serialization.py", line 1118, in deserialize_iso
    raise ValueError("Invalid datetime string: " + attr)
ValueError: Invalid datetime string: 2017-05-26 00:06:17Z
```
This is a server side issue which is being addressed, I will close this issue once it's resolved.

issue_title: Does this package support Python 3?
issue_body:
On 19 February 2017, Python 3 will be 3,000 days old!  We are interested to see if we can get **at least 50%** of the [Top 5,000 PyPI packages](http://python3wos.mybluemix.net/5000) to compatible with Python 3 by that date.  We are *really* close and given that this package is in the PyPI Top 5,000, we seek your assistance in pushing us over that threshold.

  So, if this package *already supports Python 3* then please consider adding an appropriate [trove classifier](https://pypi.python.org/pypi?%3Aaction=list_classifiers) "*Programming Language :: Python :: 3*" to this package's PyPI page so that tools can automaticly determine its Python 3 compatibility.

issue_title: Hook to reject or warn about case changes in filenames
issue_body: In case sensitive filesystems, you could commit two files that have the same name but with different case (`myfile.txt` vs `MyFile.txt`), but then if these are checked out into a case insensitive filesystem, the results are unpredictable. A hook to warn or reject commits that result in these sorts of filenames would be nice.

issue_title: match_info missing in cloned request
issue_body:
## Long story short

I am cloning the given request object when there is a special header `X-Method-Override` so that my users can bypass some overly-restrictive firewalls that doesn't allow HTTP methods other than GET/POST.

To implement this feature, I wrote a middleware like this:

```python
async def api_middleware_factory(app, handler):
    async def api_middleware_handler(request):
        method_override = request.headers.get('X-Method-Override', None)
        if method_override:
            request = request.clone(method=method_override)
        if request.match_info.http_exception is not None:
            return request.match_info.http_exception
        return (await handler(request))
    return api_middleware_handler
```

And I have a test case like below, using pytest-aiohttp plugin:

```python
async def test_api_method_override(test_client):
    inner_request = None
    app = web.Application()

    async def dummy_handler(request):
        nonlocal inner_request
        inner_request = request
        return web.Response(body=b'test')

    app.router.add_post('/v{version:\d+}/version', dummy_handler)
    app.middlewares.append(api_middleware_factory)
    client = await test_client(app)

    resp = await client.post('/v2/version', headers={
        'X-Method-Override': 'REPORT',
    })
    assert resp.status == 200
    assert inner_request.method == 'REPORT'
```

## Expected behaviour

The test case passes: `match_info` is cloned as well, so checking `request.match_info.http_exception` should be fine.

## Actual behaviour

The test case fails with:
```
Error handling request
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/joongi/Projects/Lablup/backend.ai-manager/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp/web_protocol.py", line 422, in start
    resp = yield from self._request_handler(request)
  File "/Users/joongi/Projects/Lablup/backend.ai-manager/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp/web.py", line 306, in _handle
    resp = yield from handler(request)
  File "/Users/joongi/Projects/Lablup/backend.ai-manager/ai/backend/gateway/server.py", line 73, in api_middleware_handler
    if request.match_info.http_exception is not None:
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'http_exception'
```
It seems that `match_info` is missing in the cloned request.

I also found [a suspective code of aiohttp](http://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/aiohttp/web_middlewares.html?highlight=match_info) which explicitly retry resolving of the request object after cloning and assigning the private member `_match_info` of the cloned request with the new resolve result. Is this the only way for my use-case or am I missing something?

## Steps to reproduce

Simplified sample codes are given above.

## Your environment

macOS 10.13, Python 3.6.3 via homebrew + pyenv, aiohttp 2.2.5

issue_title: Remove non-ascii characters from PEM file.
issue_body:
Apparently some JVMs are very strict about non-ascii characters, even in comments. See kennethreitz/requests#3776 for more.

This can be fixed by piping the download through a small Python script:

```python
import sys

for l in sys.stdin.readlines():
    print unicode(l, 'utf8').strip().encode('ascii', errors='backslashreplace')
```

issue_title: error on saving notebooks whose path contains %5C (\)
issue_body:
On windows, when a notebook in a sub directory is opened with an url in which `\` (backslash) escaped as `%5C` is used instead of `/` (slash) as path separator, jupyter throws the following error upon save:

> Unexpected error while saving file: B\notebook.ipynb [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'C:\\A\\.ipynb_checkpoints\\B\\notebook.ipynb'

Where `jupyter notebook` runs in directory A and the notebook files lives in its sub-directory B.

Likely bc the file should be: `C:\\A\\B\\.ipynb_checkpoints\\notebook.ipynb`
Leads to error: `http://localhost:8888/notebooks/B%5Cnotebook.ipynb`
Does not: `http://localhost:8888/notebooks/B/notebook.ipynb`
This is an real issue because nbopen opens files using backslash paths (could easily be changed there, but I believe a fix here is much more appropriate). Best, J

issue_title: Add a disable extensions option to easily run the notebook without any extensions
issue_body:
Extensions are frequently the cause of problems in the notebook. The first step to checking this is re-launching with extensions disabled, but this is pretty hard to do, as they can come from so many places.

issue_title: Compatibility with Python 2.7
translation-required on every exceptions
issue_body:
I like to distinguish 2 types of errors:

* User errors, which concern the user and are more or less nicely displayed to her (`UserError`, `ValidationError`, ...)
* Exception errors which concern the developers and are either logged, either caught by a `try: except:` reraising a `UserError`, either left as a connector job error, ...

While the first type of errors is surely translated, I dislike to translate the exception errors, that's a pain for the developers to search for translated errors.

Actual:

The `translation-required` lint actually requires every exception raised to be translated.

https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/master/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L443-L460

Expected:

The `translation-required` lint requires only the Odoo Exceptions (`UserError`, `ValidationError`) raised to be translated.

issue_title: Handle undefined bibtex strings
issue_body: The idea is to be able to represent undefined bibtex strings as such even if the definition does not appear in the current bibdata. This would be an alternative to including and interpolating common strings and would enable exporting bibtex files identical to the input even when they include things such as `month = jan`.

issue_title: pysc2.bin.play: mod or map dependencies...no longer available
issue_body:
I am able to run replays from the replay pack just fine using:

`python -m pysc2.bin.play --replay <path/to/replay>`

But when I try to run the same command on a personal replay from a 1v1 ladder match, I get the following error: 

`SC2APIProtocol.ResponseStartReplay.Error.LaunchError: 'The  launch of this game makes reference to mod or map dependencies which are no longer available.'`

Full console dump [here](https://gist.github.com/loganjhennessy/c831cb5428c449d60f9be047bd68e82b).

Some more specifics. I'm running the pysc2 command from an Ubuntu 16.04 VM, the replays were generated on Battle.net using the Windows client.

Things I've eliminated:

- Game version miss-match. This is not it. The version of my personal replay was 3.16.1.55958, and I was able to find and run replays of the same version from the replay pack.
- Missing map. Also not it. I tried several replays with different maps, again I verified I could find and run a replay with the same map from  the replay pack.

So, this leaves two things that I can think of right now.

1. Something about attempting to run a replay generated on Windows via the Linux client. But, if this is the case, then how were the replays in the replay pack generated? Obviously those weren't played on Linux, so did they undergo some kind of preprocessing?
2. A flag that must be set when attempting to run a replay that isn't from the replay pack. This will be my next line of investigation.

I guess I'd just like to know if running replays generated from other systems is possible, and if not then is it even possible using the open-source tools that have been provided?

For context, I'd like to build a strategy analyzer app using supervised learning. To analyze someone's strategy, I'll need to accept their replay regardless of what system it was generated on. If I can't do that, it's a bit of a non-starter.

I'll be posting this to the  [Starcraft II API Forum](https://us.battle.net/forums/en/sc2/22814033/) as well.

Thanks.

issue_title: application-package-names doesn't work with namespaced packages
issue_body:
Hi,

The `application-package-names` doesn't seem to work properly with namespaced packages.

E.g.
```
# flake8-import-order will consider this as a third-party package, and disallow newlines
application-package-names = mynamespace.myproject
```

This is probably because the `root_package_name` function considers `mynamespace` to be the root package name.

https://github.com/PyCQA/flake8-import-order/blob/b563bc958d37b017cb9c79ab85121299bd459137/flake8_import_order/__init__.py#L82

issue_title: Coverage of remote services
issue_body:
This is more of a question than a defect, but I'm just wondering if there is any way to use pytest-cov, maybe with the `--dist` flag, to gather metrics from code stored and executed on a remote server?

Here's the use case I have: I have a Python library I'm writing which interacts with a third party service. To test my library I have a pytest framework that launches a Docker container with the third party service installed on it, then I install my library inside the container, then I execute my test code. The testing logic then interacts with the third party service to flex the behavior of my library.

The problem here is that the Python runtime running my library isn't on my host / local machine, it's inside the docker container... and my scripts are invoked directly by the test API, but rather indirectly via the third party service running in the container.

So my question is: is there any way for me to set up the Python environment inside the Docker container to somehow run pytest and track the coverage metrics of any and all calls made to my library, and then accumulate those metrics and report them by the pytest runner running on the host?

issue_title: don't add includeSubDomains for Strict-Transport-Security header
issue_body:
Hi,

I think the letsencrypt client shouldn’t add `includeSubDomains` to the Strict-Transport-Security header by default.

If you let the letsencrypt client modify the configuration for a webserver which hosts example.com, this would make it only possible to reach subdomain hosts (foo.example.com) with https. If you forgot only one webserver which serves a subdomain by http only, you can't reach this one any more.
And as browsers actually don't have an option to clear the HSTS cache, It won't help you to remove the includeSubDomains later from the webserver config.

includeSubDomains is something, that an admin should only add if he truly knows, that all subdomains are reachable by https. So I think this should never be added by an automatic tool, like the letsencrypt client.

RFC 6797 section 11.1 [1] also describes some implications of includeSubDomains

Chris

[1] https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6797#section-11.4

issue_title: Give friendlier errors on new error types from server
issue_body:
Currently, if the client receives an unrecognized error type from the server (e.g. rate limited), it gives an unfriendly error:

```
An unexpected error occurred.
ClientError: (<Response [429]>, DeserializationError("Could not decode 'typ' (u'urn:acme:error:rateLimited'): Deserialization error: Error type not recognized",))
```

Instead, the client should expect that error types will be added that it does not recognize, and report them to the user similarly to other errors, including the error detail message.

I think this is fairly high priority to fix, because otherwise it prohibits Boulder from using meaningful error types.

issue_title: Offset saved on broker are ignored when Consumer starts
issue_body:
Hi,

I think I found an issue with the commit for consumers.
On current master of confluent-kafka-python and librdkafka, Kafka v0.10.
There are messages in the topic.

To reproduce the issue, run sample script, wait until auto commit get called (it does not raise any error). Stop by pressing CTRL+C (wait for closing).
Launch the script again, offset are not set in assignment callback. Also if we set 'auto.offset.reset': 'error', it returns KafkaError{code=_NO_OFFSET,val=-168,str="no previously committed offset available"}.



Kafka ACLs :
```
# Current ACLs for resource `Topic:kafka`:
        # User:kafka has Allow permission for operations: Write from hosts: *
        # User:kafka has Allow permission for operations: Read from hosts: *
        # User:kafka has Allow permission for operations: Describe from hosts: *

# Current ACLs for resource `Group:kafka`:
        # User:kafka has Allow permission for operations: Read from hosts: *
        # User:kafka has Allow permission for operations: Describe from hosts: *

# Current ACLs for resource `Cluster:kafka-cluster`:
        # User:kafka has Allow permission for operations: Create from hosts: *

```

Sample script :

```
from confluent_kafka import Consumer, KafkaException, KafkaError
import sys
import logging
import os


librdkafka_conf = {
    'bootstrap.servers': "10.252.18.190:9092",
    # 'client.id': 'kafkaclient1',
    'group.id': "kafka",
    # 'compression.codec': "none",
    'session.timeout.ms': 6000,
    'security.protocol': "sasl_plaintext",
    "default.topic.config": {"topic.metadata.refresh.interval.ms": 20000},
    'sasl.username': "kafka",
    'sasl.password': "password",
    'sasl.mechanisms': "PLAIN",
    'statistics.interval.ms': 10000,
    'enable.partition.eof': False,
    'api.version.request': False,
    'offset.store.method': None, # Get all messages back from beginning
    'broker.version.fallback': '0.10.0.1',
    'enable.auto.commit': True,
    'auto.commit.interval.ms': 10000,
    'default.topic.config': {
        # 'auto.offset.reset': 'earliest',
        # 'auto.offset.reset': 'error', 
        # 'auto.commit.enable': False, # Not required with high level consumer
        # 'offset.store.method': "file", # By default broker is used
    }
}


class KafakConsumer():
    def __init__(self):
        librdkafka_conf['on_commit'] = self._offset_commit_cb
        librdkafka_conf['error_cb'] = self._error_cb
        self.c = Consumer(**librdkafka_conf)
        self.c.subscribe(["kafka"], on_assign=self._print_assignment, on_revoke=self._print_revoke)
        self.partitions = []
        
    def run(self):
        while True:
            msg = self.c.poll(timeout=30.0)
            if msg is None or msg.error():
                # Error or event
                if msg is None:
                    logging.debug("Timeout while waiting for message")
                elif  msg.error().code() == KafkaError._PARTITION_EOF:
                    continue
                elif msg.error():
                    # Error
                    logging.exception(KafkaException(msg.error()))
            else:
                # Proper message
                payload = msg.value()

    
    def _error_cb(self, kafka_error):
        logging.critical("Got error from kafka : %s"%kafka_error.str())


    def _offset_commit_cb(self, err, partitions):
        logging.info("Autocommit cb Position : %s"%self.c.position(partitions))
        if err and err.code() != KafkaError.NO_ERROR:
            logging.critical("Offset committed for partitions %s, result %s"%(partitions, err.str()))
        else:
            logging.info("Offset committed for partitions %s"%(partitions))

    def _print_assignment (self, consumer, partitions):
        logging.warning('Assignment: %s'%partitions)
        self.partitions = partitions
        logging.info("Assignment position : %s"%self.c.position(self.partitions))


    def _print_revoke (self, consumer, partitions):
        logging.warning('Revoke: %s'%partitions)

    def exit(self):
        logging.info("Exit position : %s"%self.c.position(self.partitions))
        try:
            self.s.close()
        except:
            pass

issue_title: Best way to run in environments without connectivity to Salesforce API?
issue_body:
This plugin is working great for us and we have an inquiry/documentation request. 

**Question:** What is the best way to run a django server that includes django-salesforce and no available Salesforce instance?

**Background:** We've been exploring is running our apps for development and CI/CD purposes when there is either no access to a Salesforce API or even no access to internet entirely (no active network interfaces except localhost). The dream is that we can run our apps and continue developing in these rare case we have no internet - like on a beach somewhere remote or on an airplane without internet on the way to getting to that beach.

**Possible Solutions?:** My understanding is that one option is to point to a local instance of sqllite. Another option we've looked into is disabling the System Check Framework for the app where we use django-salesforce.

Currently if we disconnect from all internet and try to run our apps we get stuck with 'Performing system checks...' How do we move beyond this issue?

issue_title: utilities.py/_isnan(values) raised TypeError
issue_body:
#### _isnan return TyepError
When I tried follows codes, TypeError occured.

```python
map = folium.Map(location=[26.200627, 127.757604])
folium.PolyLine(locations=data[['latitude','longitude']].values).add_to(map)
# data is pandas DataFrame which has 'latitude' and 'logitude'
```
```error
TypeError: only length-1 arrays can be converted to Python scalars```
```
This error was caused by using math module in utilities.py/_isnan ()

``` python
def _isnan(values):
    """Check if there are NaNs values in the iterable."""
    return any(math.isnan(value) for value in _flatten(values))
```

So I suggest you modify it as follows
``` python
def _isnan(values):
    """Check if there are NaNs values in the iterable."""
    return np.isnan(values).any()
```

issue_title: Nessted schema: Inherit session from parent.
issue_body:
Deserialization of nested objects raises: `ValueError: Deserialization requires a session`. Example below:

```
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, backref
from marshmallow import fields
from marshmallow_sqlalchemy import field_for, ModelSchema


class Student(Model):
    __tablename__ = "students"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    age = Column(Integer)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False)

class Account(Model):
    __tablename__ = "accounts"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    balance = Column(Integer, default=0)

    student_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("students.id"))
    student = relationship("Student", backref="accounts")

class AccountSchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = Account


class StudentSchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = Student

    accounts = fields.Nested(
        AccountSchema, only=("id", "balance"), many=True
    )

data = {
    "name": "Python", "age": 17,
    "accounts": [{"balance": 100}, {"balance": 150}] 
}
obj, errors = StudentSchema(session=self.db.session).load(data)


```
The problem can be solve by passing session from parent schema inside the property 'schema' of class 'Nested' (file: fields.py, lines: 405-420). Or maybe there is any other solution?

issue_title: Set eol default to CR or CRLF based on platform introspection?
issue_body:
Copy-paste into a Micropython terminal works well with GNU screen, but miniterm has a weird artifact when copy-pasting that I would like to diagnose and fix.

Micropython paste mode, (which suppresses python auto-indent) is successfully triggered by CTRL+E in both screen and miniterm.  

It presents as comprehensible and usable with GNU Screen, as invoked like `screen /dev/ttyUSB0 115200`...

```python
>>> 
paste mode; Ctrl-C to cancel, Ctrl-D to finish
=== from time import sleep
=== from machine import Pin
=== from vgkits.ws2811 import *
=== 
>>> 
```

However, for some reason, a paste into Miniterm from within the same desktop terminal, and to the same UART shell on the same device, adds extra newlines or carriage returns into the terminal when copy-pasting, which has the effect of junking the readability of the pasted code. For example, this session invoked like`python3 -m serial.tools.miniterm --raw --encoding ascii /dev/ttyUSB0 115200`.

```python
--- Miniterm on /dev/ttyUSB0  115200,8,N,1 ---
--- Quit: Ctrl+] | Menu: Ctrl+T | Help: Ctrl+T followed by Ctrl+H ---

>>> 
paste mode; Ctrl-C to cancel, Ctrl-D to finish
=== from time import sleep
=== 
from machine import Pin
=== 
from vgkits.ws2811 import *
=== 

>>> 
``` 

Is there some flag I can use to eliminate this behaviour, or is it somehow to do with the way Miniterm actually sets up pipes of characters and is somehow baked into the system? 

# BACKGROUND QUESTION

I would really prefer to use miniterm compared to GNU Screen, for cross-platform compatibility as part of support packages (mostly authored in python) as part of the [vgkits.org](https://vgkits.org) initiative.

For example, I can wrap miniterm easily from within my tool suite.

```python
import sys
from serial.tools import miniterm
import config, command

minicomCommand = "serial.tools.miniterm --raw --encoding ascii ${port} ${baud}"
minicomLookup = config.hardwareConfig()
command.emulateInvocation(minicomCommand, minicomLookup)
miniterm.main()
```

Unfortunately, copy-pasting from a text-editor into the shell for interactive debugging and learning is part of the core way of working I would like to share with learners of python. Is use of Miniterm and use of paste mode across platforms destined to fail?

issue_title: training_iterations in .mlmodel is always 1 when using GPU on macOS 10.14
issue_body:
Here's my scenario:

1. Using dataset from here: https://lear.inrialpes.fr/people/marszalek/data/ig02/
1. Following this: https://apple.github.io/turicreate/docs/userguide/object_detection/data-preparation.html for data preparation/generating SFrame
1. Following this: https://apple.github.io/turicreate/docs/userguide/object_detection/ to train the model

Training with turicreate 5.0b1 on a Late 2013 Macbook Pro with GT 750M. As expected, it is using GPU by default, doing 5000 iterations, and everything looks fine in the output, except that when used in a CoreML app, the model doesn't recognise anything.

Opening the .mlmodel file in an editor reveals that there's a line having `training_iterations1�`. At the same time, the .mlmodel file from [this example](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/vision/recognizing_objects_in_live_capture), has it as `training_iterations4000�` there, which made me suspect there's something wrong. I have then tried to re-run the training with `max_iterations` set to 15, but the output still had 1 (although `max_iterations` was 15 inside .mlmodel, as expected).

However, using `tc.config.set_num_gpus(0)` to force CPU-only computation yielded the correct result: both `training_iterations` and `max_iterations` was 15.

So it looks like a GPU rendering issue, but I am not sure if I there's any way to actually detect when and why it happens. On a side note, I am also experiencing [Issue #88](https://github.com/apple/turicreate/issues/88)

# coding: utf-8
from coreapi.codecs.jsondata import JSONCodec

issue_title: `Field` parameters not in spec
issue_body:
[From the source](https://github.com/core-api/python-client/blob/master/coreapi/document.py#L45-L49):

```
# The field class, as used by Link objects:

# NOTE: 'type', 'description' and 'example' are now deprecated,
#       in favor of 'schema'.
Field = namedtuple('Field', ['name', 'required', 'location', 'schema', 'description', 'type', 'example'])
```

But [the spec](http://www.coreapi.org/specification/document/) for _Link Parameters_ (which are the same thing as `Field` here right?) doesn't mention anything beyond 'name', 'required', and 'location'. 

Q: What's the format of the `schema` parameter? Is this documented? 

Thanks.

issue_title: The training speed of gluon-ssd is slower than that of mxnet-ssd (39 vs 120 images / second)
issue_body:
Hi,
It's a great work.
The mAP of ssd_300_vgg16_atrous_voc reaches 0.77.

However, I found the training speed of gluon-ssd is slower than that of [mxnet-ssd](https://github.com/zhreshold/mxnet-ssd).
 
I use Tesla M40 24GB to train SSD(VGG16, 300 x 300) model.

# Environment
Operator System: Ubuntu 14.04
GPU: Tesla M40 24GB
Python: 2.7.12
MXNet: 1.2.0 (installed by pip)
GluonCV: 0.1.0 (installed by pip)


# Performance
Name | #GPUS|Training Speed(images / second)
---------|-----------|-----------
SSD in GluonCV|1|30
SSD in GluonCV|2|37
SSD in GluonCV|4|39
SSD in GluonCV|8|40
mxnet-ssd|4|120
SSD in Caffe|4|40

Reading 32 images takes 5e-5 second in #worker is 32.
It seems that the bottleneck is [data parallel](https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-cv/blob/master/scripts/detection/ssd/train_ssd.py#L175).

[net.target_generator](https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-cv/blob/master/scripts/detection/ssd/train_ssd.py#L183) takes 0.13~0.8 sec times the number of devices. It is not parallel due to [python loop](https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-cv/blob/master/scripts/detection/ssd/train_ssd.py#L175).

issue_title: Pareto Front Exploration
issue_body:
**z3 version:** 5bc4c9809e232d63f46018b200cb930bca993ce5

**issue:** cannot retrieve multiple pareto-front solutions

**steps to reproduce:**

The following formula:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-option:opt.priority par)
(declare-fun a () Real)
(declare-fun b () Real)
(declare-fun c () Real)

(assert (= c 5))

(assert (or
	(and (= a 1) (= b 1))
	(and (= a 2) (= b 1))
	(and (= a 1) (= b 2))
))
(maximize (+ a c))
(maximize b)

(check-sat)
(get-objectives)
(get-objectives)
(check-sat)
(get-objectives)
(get-objectives)
(check-sat)
(get-objectives)
(get-objectives)
```

has a pareto-front comprised of `2` solutions. 

According to comments [here](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/issues/1419), the correct approach to retrieve multiple pareto-front solutions in the newest version of `z3` is to invoke `check-sat` multiple times, and each time use the commands `get-objectives` and `get-model` to extract the pareto-front solution.

The output of the tool, however, does not seem to change upon multiple `check-sat` invocations:

```
sat
(objectives
 ((+ a c) 7)
 (b 1)
)
(objectives
 ((+ a c) 7)
 (b 1)
)
sat
(objectives
 ((+ a c) 7)
 (b 1)
)
(objectives
 ((+ a c) 7)
 (b 1)
)
sat
(objectives
 ((+ a c) 7)
 (b 1)
)
(objectives
 ((+ a c) 7)
 (b 1)
)
```

**Q:** so how do I extract all pareto-front solutions?
 
**related questions:**

**0.** Is the new syntax documented somewhere?

**1.** is `z3` supposed to print `unsat` when the solutions on the Pareto front are all exhausted? or is it supposed to roll-over and start from the beginning? or is it supposed to simply print `sat` and the last solution again?

**2.** `push`, `pop`, `assert`, `assert-soft`, `minimize` and `maximize` are all commands that can alter the state of an ongoing Pareto optimization search. Is the Pareto search being reset when one of these commands is issued? are there any other commands that reset the Pareto search?

issue_title: Rename MultipleInputsLayer
issue_body:
It has been noted a [few](https://github.com/benanne/Lasagne/issues/104#issuecomment-73141174) [times](https://groups.google.com/d/topic/lasagne-users/P_OakWeHwj0/discussion) that `MultipleInputsLayer` is easily mistaken to be related to `InputLayer`.

One possibility would be renaming it to `MultipleIncomingLayer`, since that reflects the keyword used to link layers to their incoming layers. This is not a whole lot better, though. `MultiLayer` could work, but might be a bit unspecific.

Another possibility is to focus on the aspect of taking multiple inputs and merging them in some way, as the class docstring acknowledges:
```python
class MultipleInputsLayer(Layer):
    """
    This class represents a layer that aggregates input from multiple layers.
    It should be subclassed when implementing new types of layers that
    obtain their input from multiple layers.
    """
```
After all, it allows multiple incoming layers, but always produces a single output, so it must do some kind of aggregation. Some ideas on this theme: `MergeLayer`, `MergingLayer`, `AggregateLayer`, `AggregationLayer`, `JoinLayer`, `JoiningLayer`, `CombinationLayer`, ...

This should be discussed and/or addressed before the first release.

issue_title: Convert InputLayer input_shape to tuple if needed
issue_body:
At least one user got trapped when passing a shape list instead of a shape tuple to the `InputLayer` constructor: https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/commit/bc963cd12b2b7b51d81225c50077521d40aee75c#commitcomment-19594546
Many `get_output_shape_for()` implementations do not rely on the shape being a tuple, but if they do, it is hard to understand where the problem originated.
We should probably just unconditionally convert the shape to a tuple in the `InputLayer` constructor.

issue_title: Http client
issue_body:
Hi, 
I am thinking about creating a HTTP (web) interface for MPSYT.

To do this, I plan on using Flask to create a web server (using python) and reimplenting the UI with Javascript/HTML/CSS. The client would then communicate with MPSYT via Ajax or web sockets. 

As planned, the flow would look like this:
1. Start server (I.e. `mpsyt server`)
2. Web interface opens and checks for current state of player (current tracks, queue, etc.) 
3. User opens web interface and browses for videos and playlists
4. Web client pushes new queue back to the server

The main benefits of this approach is that full functionality is maintained while playing tracks and that anyone on the network can control the player (at the same time). 

Any thoughts on this? Any preference on which port number to use? Is there anyone that can assist me with the development of this (I am unfamiliar with the MPSYT code base). 

Thanks!

ganga misses updates of self-built packages
issue_body:
I quite often run into problems when checking out (DaVinci) packages while a ganga session is running. The running ganga session does not -after building the new packages- send the python modules and shared libraries of the new package to new grid jobs. How I usually end up in this situation is:

1. log in to lxplus in terminal (A)
2. `SetupDaVinci --build-env vVVrRR`
3. `getpack Phys/MYPACKAGE vVVrRR`
4. `cd Phys/MYPACKAGE/cmt ; cmt config ; cmt make -j8`
5. open new terminal (B)
6. in terminal (B) `SetupGanga ; lhcb-proxy-init` (password) `ganga`
7. submit a DaVinci grid job (with `masterpackage="Phys/MYPACKAGE"`)
8. job finds the configurables from MYPACKAGE but fails since I should use a more recent version of `Phys/OFFICIALPACKAGE` (new property added to a DecayTreeTupleTool)
9. go back to terminal (A) `cd ../../.. ; getpack Phys/OFFICIALPACKAGE head ; cd Phys/MYPACKAGE/cmt ; cmt br cmt config ; cmt br cmt make -j8`
10. observe, that also OFFICIALPACKAGE gets rebuilt; check that in `~/cmtuser/DaVinci_vVVrRR/InstallArea` the library for OFFICIALPACKAGE is present
11. check that in `~/cmtuser/DaVinci_vVVrRR/InstallArea/path/to/python/module/of/configurable` the new property is listed
12. go to terminal (B), create a new DaVinci job in ganga (i.e. not cloning or copying the old job)
13. submit the new job, see it fail for the same old reason

rerunning makes and configures in terminal (A) can be done in all sorts of combinations without success. the only way i found so far is to close ganga in terminal (B), close the ssh session in terminal (B), log back in to lxplus and redo steps 6 and 7.

So far i always thought that at the start of ganga the list of present *projects* gets checked (as in, i always failed in the past when doing step 5 before step 4 finished) but it took me once again hours to realise that ganga doesn't even reinvestigate the contents of InstallAreas whenever a new job object gets instantiated.

(This is using v601r18 and v600r44)

issue_title: Documentation example for TOTP is misleading
issue_body:
Quoting usage documentation:
```
totp = pyotp.TOTP('base32secret3232')
totp.now() # => '492039'

# OTP verified for current time
totp.verify('492039') # => True
time.sleep(30)
totp.verify('492039') # => False
```

This implies that password is valid for 30 seconds after its generation which is not true. When verifying the password this way, it's valid only until the time interval for which it was generated ends.

I suggest to either explicitly state this or change the example to include `valid_window` option.

issue_title: proxy mode: add support for disabling head insert and banner
issue_body:
Proxy mode does not include `wombat.js`, however, the head insert is still added by default and the banner is still displayed. Add the following options, which default to true.

```
proxy:
    ...
    use_banner: true
    use_head_insert: true
```

- If `use_banner` is set to false, the banner in `banner.html` will not be added. (A custom banner can override this property, which is checked in the Jinja2 template)

- If `use_head_insert` is false, no modifications are made to the page, `head_insert.html` is not inserted at all.

issue_title: Allow user to select configuration file
issue_body:
Pulp Smash's configuration file does not live with the source code itself. This has the advantage that Pulp Smash can be installed in one of many ways, yet the test suite can still be called in an indentical manner. It doesn't matter whether you:

* Install system-wide by executing `sudo pip install pulp_smash` or `sudo python setup.py install`
* Install on a per-user basis with `python setup.py install --user`
* Install into a virtualenv
* Run directly from source code

In all of these cases, you can execute the exact same command to run the test suite: `python -m unittest discover pulp_smash.tests`. And in all of these cases, the same config file can be used. There's no need to do something crazy like `sudo vim /usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pulp_smash/config.json`!

There's one down-side to the current approach, though: it's hard to work with multiple configuration files. Imagine that a user creates two virtualenvs, installs Pulp Smash into each, and then wants to run Pulp Smash from both locations. How can the user make the two Pulp Smash installations use a different configuration file? Right now, it's impossible to do without resorting to something like this:

```sh
vim ~/tmp/settings.json
XDG_CONFIG_DIRS='~/tmp/' python -m unittest2 discover pulp_smash.tests
```

And that *works*, but gosh darn it's kinda terrible.

Let's add proper support for multiple configuration files. The end goal is the ability to execute commands like this:

```sh
python -m pulp_smash run --config ~/tmp/settings.json
python -m pulp_smash run --config config1.json
python -m pulp_smash run --config config2.json
```

The first command would make Pulp Smash use `~/tmp/settings.json`. The second and third commands would make Pulp Smash search through the `$XDG_CONFIG_DIRS` for files named `config1.json` and `config2.json`, respectively. If this procedure sound confusing, just think of how the shell executes commands. If you type `/usr/bin/df`, the shell executes `/usr/bin/df`. If you type `df`, the shell searches through each path in `$PATH` and uses the first executable named `df` it finds.

All this assumes that the test suite can be run by executing `python -m pulp_smash run`. That's a whole 'nuther issue.

issue_title: Linux x86 builds - OS dependencies issues
issue_body:
Currently, we have a handful of packages which fail for x86 builds on Travis.  Errors look like this: 
```
In file included from /usr/include/curl/curl.h:35:0, from /home/conan/.conan/data/Azure-C-Shared-Utility/1.0.41/bincrafters/stable/build/e77dff23066e0a9b35ee98e3dea7ddca38d50003/azure-c-shared-utility-2017-08-11/adapters/httpapi_curl.c:14:

/usr/include/curl/curlrules.h:142:3: error: size of array '__curl_rule_01__' is negative
   __curl_rule_01__
   ^
CMakeFiles/aziotsharedutil_dll.dir/build.make:1046: recipe for target 'CMakeFiles/aziotsharedutil_dll.dir/adapters/httpapi_curl.c.o' failed
```

@uilianries identified that the problem exists when building for arch=x86 on an x64 machine (such as travis), when the build requires linking to existing OS packages on the build machine.  This is because all the OS packages installed on the VM are the x64 variant, which will cause errors when you try to link to them dynamically during x86 compilation. 

One workaround would be to uninstall any x64 packages and re-install the x86 variant before we try to build. This would be ugly. 

Alternatively, docker should make this relatively easy to fix, since Docker images are already used extensively in conan_package_tools.  We just need any x86 build variants to automatically run on x86 docker images with all x86 OS packages installed. 

At least, this is our current line of thinking.  Does this sound like a good approach?  If so, how hard would it be to create copies of lasotes existing docker images but starting from x86 images?

issue_title: Support glob or regex patterns for the --logdir flag
issue_body:
Currently, it can be difficult to use TensorBoard with many experiments:

- Ideally, I would just point TensorBoard to my root log directory. Then I could filter different sets of experiments by regex via the UI. However, TensorBoard becomes unusably slow for more than about a hundred runs. While this would be great to solve in the long term, I assume there is no easy fix here.
- One workaround is to manually select the sub directories that match a certain regex and pass them as a list to TensorBoard. The problem is that every time I start a new experiment that matches the pattern, I need to restart TensorBoard to include the new experiment.
- Grouping each set of experiments I want to compare into a separate sub directory is not possible, because the sets of experiments we need to compare overlap. Copying the log directory into multiple places is not tractable while experiments are running and symlinks are not available for CNS.

It would be extremely useful if TensorBoard supported glob or regex patterns for the `--logdir` argument on the command line. This way, one could start TensorBoard instances for a certain subset of experiments and later experiments matching the pattern would be included.

I think this is a relatively small change, that would make TensorBoard a lot more useful for analyzing many experiments.

issue_title: Bin model of "Advances in Pre-Training Distributed Word Representations"
issue_body:
I know that this is not exactly and "Issue" and maybe shouldn't be opened here in Github. But still I think that could be interesting for a lot of people and I've posted the same [here ](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/fasttext-library/QGLaGU7hn-U)and received no answers, so:

Hi,

According to this paper [Mikolov, T., Grave, E., Bojanowski, P., Puhrsch, C., & Joulin, A. (2017). Advances in Pre-Training Distributed Word Representations. arXiv preprint arXiv:1712.09405](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Farxiv.org%2Fpdf%2F1712.09405.pdf&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNExe_6uHQIED8YdqCXt0hpH4Y_9fA) the main result of their work is the new set of publicly available pre-trained models. 

They state that _"The models trained on the Wikipedia and News corpora, and on the Common Crawl, were published at the fasttext.cc website and are available to the NLP researchers."_

I think they are referring to those available [here under "English word vectors"](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Ffasttext.cc%2Fdocs%2Fen%2Fenglish-vectors.html&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNHzfcH3p9B9fCvedrQSMZ7Uyyn1_A) and [here under "Wiki word vectors". 
](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Ffasttext.cc%2Fdocs%2Fen%2Fpretrained-vectors.html&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNGxharr2PX3cS6aieOAcobcrV6Blg)
My question is that the ones under "Wiki word vectors" come with the .vec and .bin files but the ones under "English word vectors" come just with the .vec file.

Is it possible to get the .bin file of the pre-trained word vectors that they're talking about in the paper? Or just the .vec file?

Thanks!

Extra:

With word2vec the .bin file could be converted into a .vec file. I think this is because the .bin file in word2vec contains just the same information as the .vec file.

Is it possible to do this conversion with FastText?

I think maybe it's not possible because according to the documentation:
> model.bin is a binary file containing the parameters of the model along with the dictionary and all hyper parameters. The binary file can be used later to compute word vectors or to restart the optimization.

So the .bin file contains more information than the .vec but anyway maybe it's possible. I'd be grateful if you could throw some light on this.

issue_title: .vec and .bin file disagree when using -pretrainedVectors
issue_body:
Hi.

So, my understanding is that the .vec file contains word vectors for the in-vocabulary items in the .bin file.  However, this seems to not be the case when I train a model with -pretrainedVectors

**Without -pretrainedVectors:**

```
$ fasttext skipgram -input ./data -output model0 -epoch 100 -thread 40

$ grep "^test " model0.vec
test 1.1997 -1.8285 -3.239 -3.1334 1.1499 [...]
$ echo "test" | fasttext print-word-vectors model0.bin
test 1.1997 -1.8285 -3.239 -3.1334 1.1499 [...]
```

**With -pretrainedVectors**

(There's also a difference in -dim, but I doubt this is the problem.  Maybe it is though.)

```
$ fasttext skipgram -input ./data -output ./model1 -dim 300 -epoch 100 -thread 40 -pretrainedVectors glove.42B.300d.txt

$ grep "^test " model1.vec
test -0.51886 -2.1325 1.9213 1.0252 -2.2538 [...]
$ echo "test" | fasttext print-word-vectors model1.bin
test -0.05801 0.015905 0.014907 0.004234 -0.016922 [...]
```

I don't know if this is a bug, or I just don't understand what these files are supposed to do.  Thought it was worth noting though.

Thanks!

issue_title: LogDNAHandler.emit fails with non-keyword arguments
issue_body:
Hi. I've tried to add LogDNAHandler to logging configuration of some existing project and noticed that it started raise a lot of exceptions. If the record was logged with non-keyword arguments, like

    logger.debug("User %s is now %s", username, status)

Then logger would fail with `TypeError: tuple indices must be integers or slices, not str`. Still, that's a valid and reasonably common way to use standard library logging.

This happens because:

    opts = {}
    if 'args' in record:
        opts = record['args']
    ...
    if 'level' in opts:
        message['level'] = opts['level']

And `record["args"]` is a list, e.g.:

    {
        "args": ["drdaeman", "active"],
        "message: "User drdaeman is now active",
        "msg": "User %s is now %s",
        "levelname": "DEBUG",
        "levelno": 10,
        ....
    }

I know, the structured logging and keyword arguments are the way to go, but legacy logging code and quick hacks (to print some debug data quick) are reality.

Also, I'm not sure it is a good idea to use `record.args` at all - in standard library's `LogRecord`, its sole purpose is to convert from `msg` to `message`. Maybe this logic should be put behind some `options["use_args"]` flag.

issue_title: Support for converting long type in metadata for SendGrid
issue_body:
Reporting an error? It's helpful to know:

* Anymail version - 0.11.1
* ESP (Mailgun, SendGrid, etc.) - SendGrid
* Versions of Django, requests, python - Django 1.8
* Exact error message and/or stack trace

If you pass a metadata in the email that is detected by python as long, you get a 400 from the sendgrid API.  Sendgrid requires metadata to be string and it works fine if the type is detected as int or float, but doesn't work if its long. Casting those to int would solve the problem, but its better if it detects long and converts to string.
```
    def set_metadata(self, metadata):
        # SendGrid requires custom_args values to be strings -- not integers.
        # (And issues the cryptic error {"field": null, "message": "Bad Request", "help": null}
        # if they're not.)
        # We'll stringify ints and floats; anything else is the caller's responsibility.
        self.data["custom_args"] = {
            k: str(v) if isinstance(v, (int, float)) else v
            for k, v in metadata.items()
        }
```

>> Add long in isinstance

issue_title: Problem with syncing and handling a non-color value as color metadata for a calendar
issue_body:
This is linked to https://github.com/pimutils/khal/issues/596.

## Description:
When setting up a new iCloud hosted calendar with vdirsyncer, I had the experience of it syncing down a metadata file for the calendar's color with the string contents of "`None`". I have since not been able to reproduce this behavior, but based on the debug log (posted below), this value still seems to exist server-side at least. The syncing of this value caused a crash in `ikhal` (see issue mentioned at beginning of this post) due to not being able to parse the value of "`None`" from the color metadata file. 

Steps (this is as precise as I can recall):
1. This calendar was created in [Fantastical 2 on OS X](http://flexibits.com/fantastical), and was assigned a color when it was created. 
2. I closed Fantastical and then went to modify the configuration file for `vdirsyncer` for it to discover and start syncing this calendar. The initial `vdirsyncer discover` and `vdirsyncer sync` worked successfully.
3. At this point I was able to view the calendar in `ikhal` without issue but it lacked a color.
4. I created the file `color` locally in this calendar's directory to give it color metadata and gave it a **different** custom color.
5. After this point I ran `vdirsyncer metasync` and was still able to view it in `ikhal`.
6. I then deleted the color metadata file and ran `vdirsyncer metasync` again, to ensure that there was a different color being used on the server-side.
7. I got back a file with the contents of "None", which I didn't notice until I ran `ikhal` and it crashed when trying to parse this string.
8. I was able to replace the value in the file with my custom color again and `ikhal` correctly opens the file.

Additional Notes:
* All of these pieces of software use different storage locations for the calendar data for this specific calendar. The only consistent thing here is what is on the server.
    * Calendar.app (Apple software) seems to correctly show the custom color I picked for the cloud.
    * Fantastical 2 doesn't show any color for this specific calendar.
    * ikhal shows the **different** custom color I have selected
* In the attached log below the calendar in question has had the color metadata file remove from the filesystem prior to running in an attempt to reproduce getting `vdirsyncer` to download this bit of metadata. I am not seeing it get re-created, but based on the server response it is definitely there.
* Note that there are two different colors involved in the syncing here, I have different colors for displaying in my terminal vs on my mobile phone's calendar app. The cloud stores the color of the calendar that my phone should have, and i have a local override of the color in my `khal` config.

BuildVersion:	16D32
```

issue_title: Refactor tests to patch more correctly
issue_body:
We shouldn't really be patching in `setUp` and saving the patched mocks.

"If you use this technique you must ensure that the patching is “undone” by calling stop. This can be fiddlier than you might think, because if an exception is raised in the setUp then tearDown is not called. unittest.TestCase.addCleanup() makes this easier"
from https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/unittest.mock-examples.html

issue_title: Failures in storage.py
issue_body:
Usage: any invocation of `certbot`

Installation: `sudo pacman -S certbot` => `/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/certbot`

Error: An unexpected error occurred: ValueError: max() args is an empty sequence

Traceback: storage.py, line 682, in latest_common_version

Code:
```python
    def latest_common_version(self):
        """Newest version for which all items are available?

        :returns: the newest available version for which all members
            (``cert, ``privkey``, ``chain``, and ``fullchain``) exist
        :rtype: int

        """
        # TODO: this can raise CertStorageError if there is no version overlap
        #       (it should probably return None instead)
        # TODO: this can raise a spurious AttributeError if the current
        #       link for any kind is missing (it should probably return None)
        versions = [self.available_versions(x) for x in ALL_FOUR]
        return max(n for n in versions[0] if all(n in v for v in versions[1:]))
```

Fix:
```python
        #return max(n for n in versions[0] if all(n in v for v in versions[1:]))
        try:
                return max(n for n in versions[0] if all(n in v for v in versions[1:]))
        except:
                return 1
```

issue_title: Don't assume you're not using certbot-auto during tests
issue_body: [This line](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/v0.9.2/certbot/tests/cli_test.py#L110) assumes that if you're running tests, you're not using `letsencrypt-auto`. This is an unnecessary assumption and should be removed, ideally accompanied by more tests that `cli.cli_command` has the correct value.
